L1.name: Spell Challenge
L1.description: When targeting or damaging an enemy with a spell, you may choose to inflict Challenge on them until the end of your next turn. When an enemy that is Challenged by you provokes an attack of opportunity from you, you may instead use Abjurer's Blast against them. The heavy and precise version of this cost 1 RP, while the light version costs 0 RP.

L2.name: Abjurer's Blast
L2.description: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Arcane damage. This spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L3.name: Mana Shield
L3.description: You use your Encounter Start action to give yourself Mana Shield, which gives you Temporary HP equal to your Toughness + 3 or Focus + 3 (whichever is higher).  Wearing armor reduces this to 2/3rd, 1/3rd or 0 if wearing Light, Medium or Heavy Armor respectively. While Mana Shield is active, you do not provoke attacks of opportunity from casting Spells from enemies that are Challenged by you. Until the end of the encounter, at the start of your turn you may spend 1 mana to restore your Mana Shield to its maximum value. (With similarly reduced values for wearing armor) If you did not use this as an encounter start, you may also spend 1 AP and 1 Mana to activate this.

L4.name: Arcane Barrier II
L4.description: You create a Magical Barrier that absorbs damage until it breaks.  The barrier has HP equal to 15 + your Focus. The barrier has a Guard of 0 + your guard and any damage it takes is subtracted from the barrier’s HP. When the barrier breaks, any remaining damage is subtracted from your HP. The barrier otherwise lasts until the start of your next turn or until you take any action other than the Block action. If using the Block action while the barrier is active, the guard of the barrier also increases.  This spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity and does not protect against true damage.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Reason or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Arcane Pull
L8.description: You pull the target to an empty square next to you. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Your next Block or Dodge action against the target before the start of your next turn costs 0 RP. This Spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L1.name: Acolyte's Journey
L1.description: You gain proficiency in light armor, shields and a single common group of weapons. This group may include Channeling Weapons, even though they aren't consider common weapons. (But you only get 1 of the possible channeling weapons). You gain +5 skill points in the Religion skill. You are welcome at establishments for your religion and they are obligated to help you with lodging, advice, food and sometimes equipment.

L2.name: Cure Touch
L2.description: You heal the target for 11 + Power x 2

L3.name: Light's Care
L3.description: At the end of combat you may use 1 Encounter Conclusion action to heal a single target for 11 + Power x 2 and grant them First Aid. First Aid: The target can expend an Encounter Conclusion Action to prevent one injury to themself.

L4.name: Angel's Embrace
L4.description: You grant the target ally Angel’s Embrace , which grants a Shield against the triggering attack that blocks damage equal to 5 + (2 x Toughness). This Spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Medicine, Religion, Magic, History, Insight, Negotiation, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to either Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Miracle: Sanctuary
L8.description: You create an immobile sphere of protection in a 5ft radius that prevents all entry into and out of it. The Shield has health points equal to 20 + Toughness x 8. Attacks and Magical Effects cannot be casted out of or into the Sanctuary, which functions as line-of-sight blocking for the purposes of abilities. Sanctuary lasts until the start of your next turn unless you pay Upkeep (1 Mana). The Spell also ends when the Shield’s health points are reduced to zero. This spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L1.name: Adventurer Essentials
L1.description: You gain proficiency in 1 common weapon group. In addition, you gain proficiency in 1 common or specialized weapon groups. You gain proficiency in 1 armor category of your choosing. Alternatively, you may pick the shield or greatshield. You may use Presence Concealment.

L2.name: Power Jump
L2.description: You jump up to 60ft vertically straight up into the air.  You can additionally move 5ft horizontally afterwards.  Unless flying, you automatically land straight down from your position at the end of your turn or for 0 AP, this falling movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity and you don’t take fall damage from it. If you land inside an occupied square, you are shunted 5ft into an empty square (your original one if possible). You cannot make any non-teleport movement actions while in the air unless you’re flying, but can still take other actions (such as attacking) while airborne.

L3.name: Mana Razor
L3.description: Until the end of your turn you gain Mana Razor granting your basic melee attacks an additional 30ft range. Augmenting melee attacks with this ability makes them into ranged attacks. If using a Two-Handed melee weapon with Mana Razor, gain an additional 45ft range instead. Overcharge : You may spend 1 mana to have this also affect basic ranged attacks and to remove the Quick keyword from it.

L4.name: Steel Aura
L4.description: You gain Steel Aura , granting you 3 additional guard, but you can no longer use any RP actions besides Block and actions with the Rage keyword. You can end this effect at any time for 0 RP. This effect ends at the start of your next turn.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Efficient Spirit Control
L8.description: You reduce the cost of your next non-healing keyword action by 2 mana. This cannot reduce the mana cost below 0. Also resets your Circuits, allowing abilities with the Circuit keyword to be used for their reduced mana cost once more.

L1.name: Wind Master
L1.description: You gain Wind Mastery. You can use Glide.

L2.name: Wind Blast
L2.description: You may make a Light Attack that deals Wind against the target. On damage, you push the target back 20ft. Targets knocked into other characters or an object take damage as if they fell a distance equal to distance they failed to move.

L3.name: Downburst
L3.description: You may make a Heavy Attack that deals Wind damage against the target. On damage, the target immediately plummets to the ground and takes falling damage. This falling damage cannot be negated through the use of abilities that would normally negate falling damage.

L4.name: Wind Scatter
L4.description: You select a point within range. All targets in a 15x15 area must make a save against Potency or be pushed back 15ft away from the point you selected. Targets knocked into other characters or an object take damage as if they fell a distance equal to distance they failed to move.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Reason or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus or Power.

L8.name: Wind Buffet
L8.description: You blow wind in a 30ft, 60ft degree cone in your chosen direction. All caught in the cone must make a save against your Potency or be blown back to the end of the cone and knocked Prone. Enemies with a size smaller than you suffer a 1d4 penalty to this roll, while enemies bigger than you get a 1d4 bonus instead. Targets knocked into other characters or an object take damage as if they fell a distance equal to distance they failed to move.

L1.name: Controlled Wind
L1.description: When pushing a target back or up with a Wind Spell, you may choose to push up to the maximum distance instead of always pushing the maximum distance.

L2.name: Wind Blast II
L2.description: You may make a Light (1 AP), Precise (2 AP) or Heavy Attack (2 AP) that deals Wind damage against the target. On damage, you push the target back 20ft.

L3.name: Cyclone
L3.description: All targets in a 15x15 ft area must make a save against your Potency or be knocked airborne for 10ft, falling Prone once they land.

L4.name: Downburst II
L4.description: You may make a Heavy Attack that deals Wind damage against the target. On damage, the target immediately plummets to the ground and takes falling damage. This falling damage cannot be negated through the use of abilities that would normally negate falling damage.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: Razor Hurricane
L8.description: You create a rapid spinning hurricane with a 15x15 size and 50ft in height in the target location. All creatures caught in the hurricane immediately take Wind Light Damage and are pushed out of the Hurricane. Every creature that enters the hurricane immediately takes Wind Light Damage and must succeed at a save against your Potency or be pushed back 20ft. Leaving the hurricane does no damage and requires no save. Upkeep: You must spend 1 Mana and 2 AP at the start of each turn or the spell ends. When you pay this Upkeep, you may move the hurricane up to 20ft. Any creature that comes into contact with the hurricane during this move takes damage and must make a save as if they had entered it.

L1.name: Mission Control
L1.description: You gain proficiency in the Aetherie Sensory Station. You may use Mind Connection.

L2.name: Psychometry
L2.description: By touching the door to a room you gain information as to the physical layout of the room and objects within it as if you were able to see inside of the room.  By reading the Auras of all creatures within the room, you can also get a general idea of any creatures within, which you see as blobs of light—brighter for more powerful auras.  You can also use this effect on containers as well to get a general idea of the layout within containers like backpacks, and chests.  This ability can be used at unlimited range as long as a creature under the effect of Mind Connection touches the door or container.

L3.name: Conduit
L3.description: When Mind Connection is active, you can channel some abilities through others with Mind Connection. You cannot use conduit with abilities that require a physical object to be present, such as a Mystic Eye power or a specific weapon or abilities that require you to interact with a physical object, such as Snatch. Abilities that require your physical body to make an attack, such as Double Punch also don't work. You still must pay the costs to use the abilities, and your Mind Connection source becomes the origin point of the spell for purposes of range. Abilities with a range of Self cannot be used through Conduit. Any ability used through Conduit that isn't an Aetherie ability costs +1 AP (or RP) to use.

L4.name: Tap Cracker
L4.description: You open a door or interact with an object remotely.  You can cast this through a creature affected by Mind Connection.   Overcharge: You can pay 1 mana to magically unlock most doors and objects (DM can decide if an object is too complicated, and still make you roll a Skill Check).

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Appraise, Magic, Artifice, Perception, Insight, Stealth or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Agility.

L8.name: Spirit Circuit Crash
L8.description: You send a wave of magic energy onto the target, afflicting them with Spirit Circuit Crash— jamming their Spirit Circuits.  The target cannot use Secret Arts or any abilities that require mana until the end of their next turn.  The target may choose to take damage equal to your heavy attack damage to cancel this effect. You can cast this through a creature affected by Mind Connection.

L1.name: Expert Farmer
L1.description: You may use the Agrarian's core crafting abilities.

L2.name: Separate wheat from chaff
L2.description: You make a Light attack against all enemies in range. On damage, you push the target back 5ft. If you landed a Killing Blow, all that took damage become Shaken as well.

L3.name: Terracing
L3.description: You raise the ground around you in a 25x25ft square centered on yourself. The ground is raised upwards by 10ft. Can only be used on natural ground and cannot be used on ground that’s already been raised. Overcharge: You may spend 1 AP and 1 Mana to raise it upwards by another 10ft.

L4.name: Four Field System
L4.description: Make a crafting check. Afterwards, whenever you use an ability for crafting that you haven’t used before in this crafting session, it gains a +2 bonus to the crafting points it gives.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Farming.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Harvest Moon
L8.description: You call down a harvest moon over the area that shines in a 60ft radius around yourself.  All allies in range may spend 2 Mana at the start of their turn to heal for 15 HP. All enemies starting their turn in its range must make a Save against your potency or become slowed. You may end this ability early by choosing to make a 0 AP heavy attack against an enemy slowed by this ability. This attack must be made with a Scythe. On killing blow of that attack, you recover 1 mana. Upkeep : You must spend 1 mana at the start of your turn to continue this effect.

L1.name: Expert Alchemist
L1.description: You gain Alchemeister Core Crafting Abilities. When using Throw Potion, you may infuse it using your own mana. You spend X mana and the mana cost the target needs to pay to benefit from the potion is reduced by X (the mana cost cannot go below 0).

L2.name: Combat Chemistry
L2.description: Can only be used once per encounter.  Using your knowledge of alchemy, you jury-rig a special item that has the effect of an an elixir, poison, flask or potion with a cost of 300c or lower.  You can only make the base version of the item with no mods. The item doesn't take up burden, but must be used before the start of your next turn or it is lost. This item can only be used by you.

L3.name: Counter-Potion
L3.description: You may immediately use a Potion, Elixir, Salve, Poison or Flask that is held in your hand.  X is equal to the AP cost of the action.

L4.name: Detailed Finish
L4.description: Make a crafting check. Upgrade the dice to 2d8.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Alchemy.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: All of Creation
L8.description: Change the damage type of the target attack into any damage type except True.

L1.name: The Alchemist
L1.description: You gain Alchemist Core Crafting Abilities, and proficiency in 1 basic weapon. You can craft using non-advanced materials and base materials. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times. You can use an Alchemist's Alembic as crafting tool for alchemy crafts.

L2.name: Throw Potion
L2.description: You throw a Potion in your hands at a willing ally unit within range. The unit may pay the mana cost (if any) to receive the effect of the Potion.

L3.name: Doctor Jekyll
L3.description: You draw and either drink a Potion or Elixir, or throw one to a willing ally within 30ft who immediately drinks it.

L4.name: Light Work
L4.description: Only usable as the first crafting action. All material costs are doubled and all produced items are doubled. You may only use basic crafting actions for the rest of the craft. Make a crafting attempt.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Alchemy. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Medicine, Magic or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Quickdraw Alchemy
L8.description: You draw an Alchemy item from your combat inventory.

L1.name: Transmutation Tricks
L1.description: For 1 AP, You can create small items using transmuting to provide temporary comforts and curiosities— a cup to drink out of, a chair to sit down on, a figurine etc.  These items cannot have significant gameplay factors (such as a key or a tool).  If you’d like to create something small to give away or sell, you must utilize x amount of commodities of whatever quantity and type the GM decides. You may use your transmuter abilities a second time during a craft or co craft. You may use the following abilities:

L2.name: Transmute Effective Implement
L2.description: From the surrounding material you construct an “Effective Implement”, granting you a +5 to the skill of your choice.  You must explain how this implement will be effective towards the check using the relevant skill (example:  creating a springboard to jump higher with athletics) and the GM determines the validity or this ability has no effect.  Does not work with crafting or gathering skills. This implement lasts until you have attempted the specific check it was created for.  You may also spend 1 RP instead of 1 AP for this ability. Overcharge :  You can spend 1 additional mana to increase the skill bonus to +10.

L3.name: Alchemical Detonation
L3.description: You touch a wall, door or container in front of you, which shatters from deconstructive force, becoming destroyed.  Can destroy up to a 5ftx5ft segment of wall.  Only affects objects made out of common building materials (wood, clay, stone, iron etc), however it affects all walls made out of player abilities.  The GM determines if the object is detonateable.  You can then take a movement action to move 5ft into the space where the wall was.

L4.name: Siphon Material
L4.description: Errand. You siphon the alloyed material from an item if possible, removing the effects that the original item would’ve had. If you cannot remove the effect then the skill fails.  You remove the alloy and regain its original form, while also maintaining the form of the item minus the effect. The special material is retrieved in raw units.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain 10 Transmuter points. Pick one of the crafting disciplines, such as blacksmithing or alchemy. Whenever you use your Transmuter abilities in a craft of that discipline, you may add your transmuter points as bonus to that ability (even if it's not a roll). You must pick a different discipline than one you picked in Transmuter, but you gain an additional 5 points in your original discipline for free.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Transmute Proto Armatae
L8.description: You touch the ground, transmuting a Proto Armatae of your own design.  The Proto Armatae lifts you up and immediately encases you into its cockpit.   The Proto Armatae is 15ft tall and is a large creature that occupies a 15ftx15ft space.  All creatures and moveable objects surrounding the Proto Armatae are pushed back away from its zone of control in a burst of steam.  The Armatae also gains a temporary shield with 20 HP that disappears at the start of your next turn. You must use your own actions to cause the Proto Armatae to move and attack.  You can eject from a transmutation hatch on the top at the center of the Proto Armatae for 1 AP, in which the Proto Armatae stops functioning.  When you re-enter you must close the hatch for 1 AP before the Proto Armatae functions again.  The Proto Armatae has health equal to 15 + 5x Focus and it uses your AP and RP for actions.  You can channel your Transmuter abilities through the Proto Armatae, and Transmute Weapon can create a giant sized weapon for the Armatae to use. You may also spend 1 RP to use this ability instead of the AP cost. Only usable once.  You regain the use of this ability when you gain an Interlude Point.   Proto Armatae HP: – Evasion: 9/19 Guard: 4/14   Mini Transmutation Cannon (Right Arm) Cost: 0 AP Range: 40ft Description:  Fires a mini transmutation cannon of deconstructive energy making a light attack against a target.  Doesn’t provoke attacks of opportunity.   Humanoid Gripper (Left Arm) Description: Humanoid Gripper can make all basic attacks.  It is proficient in any transmuted weapon it holds.  Its regular melee attacks deal light weapon damage and any transmuted weapon it wields gains the benefit of that weapon category.

L1.name: Fungal Metabolite
L1.description: Due to symbiotic fungi within your body, you become resistant to all commonly known (book) poisons and may use Resist Poison. After being infected by an exotic poison or disease and surviving, that poison/disease is added to your list of resistances. Any disease or poison that you are resistant to can be used with Amoxicillin. Against a poison you are resistant to, you may use Resist Poison for 0 RP, 1 Mana instead of 1 RP.

L2.name: Vital Drain
L2.description: You make a Precise Attack against the target creature that deals Earth damage equal to their Toughness   with Full Pierce .   On damage the current host of Spore Symbiote is healed for the damage dealt. Vital Drain can be casted from the location of the host of your Spore Symbiote.

L3.name: Hyphal Force
L3.description: Your spore symbiote surges the host’s muscles with temporary power. When the host of Spore Symbiote is making a melee attack, increase the damage by your Light Attack Damage. The extra damage dealt is Earth Damage. This spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L4.name: Spore Burst
L4.description: You create an explosion of spores in front of you. Make a Heavy Attack against the target that deals Poison damage. Afterwards, knock back the target 5ft and knock yourself back 5ft. This Spell does not trigger attacks of opportunity.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Medicine, Magic or Survival.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Awareness or Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Mushroomize
L8.description: You fill your target with mycelium from your spore symbiote, removing the Downed state from them and preventing them from entering the downed State while this effect lasts. Removing Spore Symbiote from the target cancels this effect. This effect ends at the start of your next turn unless you spend 1 mana to continue the effect.  When paying upkeep, if the target is below 0 HP, heal the target for your power + 3. This effect ends if the target’s HP is reduced to negative their maximum HP amount. You can use 1 RP instead of 2 AP to use this ability.

L1.name: Angelic Presence
L1.description: You gain Angelic Presence, which gives a +1 to Presence.

L2.name: True Divine Release
L2.description: You unleash your latent angelic powers and transform into a semi-divine being for up to an hour. While transformed, you gain controlled flight and +5 movement speed. Upkeep: 2 mana After using this ability, you cannot do so again until the interlude rest phase.

L3.name: Divine Guard
L3.description: You may immediately use the Block action to guard against the triggering attack with a +5 bonus to Guard.

L4.name: Divine Might
L4.description: You make a Light Attack against the target.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: God's Blessing
L8.description: The AP cost of True Divine Release I is lowered by 4 and the mana cost upkeep is lowered to 0.

L1.name: Summon Animal
L1.description: You may use Summon Animal.

L2.name: Emergency Recall
L2.description: You give your summon 100 Damage Reduction against the attack. Afterwards, if it is still alive, it is immediately unsummoned.

L3.name: Half Restore
L3.description: You heal your summoned animal for 11 + Power x 2 HP. It also removes 1 negative effect from it. If the amount healed exceeds the target’s maximum HP, any extra HP is converted into temporary HP.

L4.name: Independent Action
L4.description: Your animal summoned through the Summon Animal ability gains the ability to act independently. It acts at initiative 0, though you may still control its action. A summon with Independent Action starts with 2 AP and 1 RP and may use those as normal. If they have an ability that costs mana, then you still have to pay that mana cost. While it is under the effects of Independent Action, you cannot command your summon through the use of your own AP. A summon targeted by this ability takes up 2 slots towards the summon limit. If this would put you past your limit, you cannot use this ability.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Animal Husbandry.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Bestial Command
L8.description: The summon may take basic actions as if it had 2 AP to spend. In addition, if your summon is under the effects of Independent Action, it gains Bestial Command until the end of the encounter, it dies or you cancel it for 0 AP. While Bestial Command lasts, its maximum AP and AP recovery are increased by 2 while your AP recovery is lowered by 2.

L1.name: Magic Resistance
L1.description: You gain a +2 Anti-Mage bonus to your saves against Spells.

L2.name: Dispel Magic
L2.description: You dispel a temporary magic effect from an area or target. All non-permanent effects within range that are created by an ability with the Spell or Enchantment keyword are affected. Spells that do not have a time limit listed or create a permanent effect such as a Geomancer's fissure are not affected. Bounded Fields and Secret Arts cannot be removed with Dispel Magic.

L3.name: Anti-Shield
L3.description: You weave a web of magic around the target. The target cannot be the target of any abilities with the Shield keyword until the start of your next turn.

L4.name: Anti-Magic Shield
L4.description: You grant the target ally 1 RP that must be spend on a basic Dodge or Block action against the triggering attack as well as Anti-Magic Shield for the triggering attack. Anti-Magic Shield reduces the damage the target ally takes by your Focus.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus or Power.

L8.name: Counterspell
L8.description: You pay RP equal to half the AP spent on the triggering Spell (rounded up) or equal to the RP spent on the triggering spell and Counter it, canceling its effect without refunding the AP. If the AP cost is higher than your RP, you cannot Counterspell it.  If used against a non-Grunt target, you must pay 1 mana for each point of Focus that the target has more than you (minimum 0).  This Spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity, but cannot be used to Counter Spells that have an AP and RP cost of 0.

L1.name: Expanded Circuits
L1.description: You gain Expanded Circuits, which gives +2 maximum mana.

L2.name: Arcane Buster
L2.description: You fire a blast of energy, making a Heavy Attack that deals Arcane Damage. This attack has the Shieldbreaker property.

L3.name: Arcane Explosion
L3.description: You make a Light Attack that deals Arcane damage against all enemies within 10ft of yourself. This Spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L4.name: Arcane Power
L4.description: You gain Arcane Power , which adds 3 damage to the damage of all your Arcane Damage rolls until the start of your next turn.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Magic Missile II
L8.description: You fire a highly accurate blast of arcane energy at the target, making a Precise Attack that deals Arcane damage against the target

L1.name: Status Assessment II
L1.description: You may use Status Assessment II.

L2.name: Unerring Aim
L2.description: When you aim at an object not being worn or wielded within your ranged weapon range, you always hit your target.

L3.name: Volley
L3.description: You make a Light attack against all targets in a 25x25ft area.

L4.name: Snap Shot II
L4.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. You do not add your focus to this roll.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain 5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Stealth, Medicine, Perception, Survival, Animal Husbandry or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness,or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus or Power.

L8.name: Multishot
L8.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against two different targets. Overcharge: You may spend X extra mana to attack 2 + X targets instead.

L1.name: Augmented Casting - Persistent Spell
L1.description: You may use Persistent Spell.

L2.name: Battle Cast
L2.description: The next Spell you cast before the end of your turn does not provoke an attack of opportunity from being casted in Melee Range of an opponent.

L3.name: Clearcasting
L3.description: The next Spell you cast this encounter has its mana cost reduced by 2. This cannot reduce the mana cost below 0.

L4.name: Presence of Mind
L4.description: You reduce the AP cost of your next Spell this turn by 2 AP.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Appraise, Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Ultimate Spell
L8.description: The next Spell you cast this turn with an AP cost lower than 5 deals true damage.

L1.name: Dimensional Armory
L1.description: You can create a pocket dimension with Dimensional Armory.  Creating the pocket dimension and establishing its door takes 1 week and costs 1 errand point.  The entry point can be any regular door that you fit through.  When the creator opens this door, they can decide instead to access the Dimensional Armory, which is a 15ft x 15ft closet.  This closet can hold up to 6 Burden worth of items.  Some magic items can be rejected by the dimensional armory (GM’s discretion), such as Divine Arms not bonded to you.  If the owner of a Dimensional Armory dies, everything inside of it appears in the next unoccupied space possible next to the owner. Any burden reducing items (such as an alchemy rig) in the armory have no effect.

L2.name: Send To Armory
L2.description: You touch an item not held or carried by another creature and immediately teleport it to your Dimensional Armory into an unoccupied space (if there is room).  If there is no space the spell fails.  Some magic items can be rejected by the dimensional armory.  Living creatures cannot be teleported into the Dimensional Armory. Overcharge:  You spend 1 minute instead of 2 AP, reducing the mana cost by 1.

L3.name: Fast Dimensional Equip
L3.description: You summon an item from your dimensional armory into your hand. You can also send a held weapon (not wielded or carried by another creature) under your full control to your Dimensional Armory and swap it with another from your Dimensional Armory.   You may use 1 RP to activate this ability. If you do, the Mana cost is reduced by 1.

L4.name: Dimensional Armory Expansion
L4.description: Your burden limit for your Dimensional Armory is increased by 4.  Ahead of time, you designate one armor, one cloak and one weapon/shield or two weapons or one two-handed weapon that are inside of your Dimensional Armory as a “dimensional set”, this process takes 15 minutes of concentration.  You can only have one dimensional set at a time.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Artifice, Magic, Stealth, Athletics or Art.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Full Dimensional Equip
L8.description: You swap your armor, cloak and weapons currently wielded with a dimensional set.  The weapon and armor you had equipped as you used to this ability become a Dimensional Set, allowing you to switch back to it.

L1.name: Armorsmith Basics
L1.description: You gain proficiency in light armor, medium armor and shields. You gain proficiency in 1 basic melee weapon and can use a smith's hammer as a bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient. You can craft armors, shields and cloaks using Iron, Tamahagane, Animal Hide and Direhide, as well as alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.

L2.name: Repair Armor
L2.description: Errand. You repair a broken armor.

L3.name: Share Armor
L3.description: Your ally may treat their guard and block as if they were wearing your armor until the end of the current creature’s turn. If multiple creatures of the same type move on the same initiative, it lasts through all of their turns. Any temporary increases to the guard/block of this armor (such as through reinforce armor) is not transferred over.

L4.name: Thermal Quench
L4.description: You make a crafting check. For each time you rolled a 5 or higher this craft, this check gains a +1 bonus.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Armorsmithing. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Riding or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness. You also gain proficiency in Heavy Armor or Greatshields.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Toughness.

L8.name: Reinforce Armor
L8.description: Until the start of your next turn, the guard and block value gained from an armor and shield you crafted and are wearing is doubled. Does not affect armor plates.

L1.name: Artificer Basics
L1.description: You gain Artificer Core Crafting Abilities You can craft using non-advanced materials and base materials. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times. You can now craft Artifices and Firearms.

L2.name: Repair Artifice
L2.description: Errand. You repair a broken artifice.

L3.name: Blackbird
L3.description: You deploy a Blackbird, a small artifice drone that outputs a grainy image to a device on your wrist.  The blackbird has a maximum range of 3000ft and can observe major movements and structures such as doors and enemies.  The drone cannot display fine movements or detail such as words in a book without being up close, but can see large signs.  The blackbird is the size of a walnut and is relatively quiet, and can only be heard by those with normal human hearing within 10 ft. The fuel shell lasts for one flight, up to 15 minutes.  If your Blackbird is lost or damaged, you must create a new one as an errand action before you can use this again.

L4.name: Overclock
L4.description: You may add 1d10 crafting points to the item. The item starts in the  Broken  condition. Cannot be used for Alchemy crafting.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Artificing. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Artifice, Magic, Flight, Linguistics, History or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Quick Artifice Reload
L8.description: You quickly reload one shell into an artifice in range using one of your shells.

L1.name: Inconspicuous
L1.description: If you're traveling stealthily, you may roll separate stealth checks even if traveling with the party. When doing this, any surprise rounds for you are determined purely off your own rolls and not your parties. During a surprise round, enemies you attack are still treated as having 0 RP even after you attack them until they start their turn. You still gain this benefit, even if your allies attacked them already.

L2.name: Death Mark
L2.description: You mentally mark a target for death by applying Death Mark, opening them up to your Assassin abilities. When you reduce a character marked by Death Mark to 0 HP or lower, you may move up to your Speed and take the Hide action for 0 RP. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L3.name: Study Target
L3.description: Your next basic attack against the target gains the Lock On keyword OR you apply Death Mark to the target. Overcharge: You may spend 2 additional AP to instead make the next basic attack gain the Sure hit and Full Pierce keywords.

L4.name: Devastating Strike III
L4.description: You make a Light (1 AP) or Heavy (2 AP) attack against the target.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Stealth, Deception, Roguecraft, Perception, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness, Cunning or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus, Power or Agility.

L8.name: Secret Art: Assassinate
L8.description: You attack the target for Heavy Damage.  If you surprised the enemy, are currently stealthed and have not been spotted by an enemy this entire encounter, this attack deals double heavy damage. You may move next to an enemy and still qualify as being stealthed and not spotted, provided you use this ability immediately after that movement ends.

L1.name: Frost Weapon
L1.description: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Frost Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare you chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

L2.name: Aurora Blade Style: Winter's Edge
L2.description: You imbue your blade with frost energy and make a precise attack that deals Frost damage. On hit, the target is slowed until the end of your next turn.

L3.name: Aurora Blade Style: Aurora Slash
L3.description: You fire slashes in a straight line creating a curtain of light which strikes the first target hit with a light attack that deals frost damage. On hit the target is slowed until the end of your next turn.  The light from the slash continues for the full range, creating a 300ft high wall that blocks vision on both sides. This wall of multicolored light last until the start of your next turn.

L4.name: Aurora Blade Style: Rimestep
L4.description: When a target is slowed by an attack with the frost keyword, you may sidestep up to your speed towards the target. If you have enough movement you can end your movement anywhere next to the target. Overcharge : You may spend 1 Mana to instead make it three times your speed.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Intimidation, Magic, History, Flight, Survival or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus.

L8.name: Aurora Blade Style Secret Art: Permafrost Guilotine
L8.description: You make a light attack against the target or spend 1 extra AP to make a heavy or precise attack that deals Frost damage. On damage, the target is rooted until the end of your next turn. Then, enter Permafrost Guillotine stance. While in this stance you can use Aurora Blade Style Secret Art: Permafrost Guillotine again. The stance lasts until the end of combat or until you enter different stance.

L1.name: Fire Stance
L1.description: You may use Tempestuous Fire Stance

L2.name: Double Palm Strike
L2.description: You attack the target for Light Damage. On damage, the target is moved up to 15ft away from you (At least 5 feet).

L3.name: Zhen Jiao
L3.description: When you move toward an enemy as part of a Martial Artist or Bajiquan ability due to Fire Stance or an inherent move effect of the ability (such as Rushing Knee Drop), your attack deals three extra points of damage for every 5ft moved. If your attack hits more than once or more than one target, you only apply this damage once.

L4.name: Elbow Uppercut
L4.description: You attack the target for Heavy Damage. Combo: If used after an ability with the Unarmed Strike keyword, this ability gains Full Pierce.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Power.

L8.name: Tetsuzanko
L8.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. This attack deals additional Light Damage added to the Heavy Damage. Combo: If used after an ability that moved the target, you may push the target for an equal distance again.

L1.name: Toss a coin
L1.description: As an Errand action, you may perform in a city or town and earn Clim equal to your relevant Art roll x 10 up to a maximum of 300 clim.

L2.name: Inspiring Song
L2.description: All allies within range (including yourself) may add 1d4 to a single roll they make until the start of your next turn. They may choose to add this after seeing the roll. Upkeep: At the start of your next turn and every turn thereafter, you may spend 1 AP to continue this performance, granting all allies within range the benefit once again.

L3.name: Dirge of Despair
L3.description: All enemies within range must make a save against your Potency or become affected by this Song. While affected, you may subtract 1d4 from a single roll they make until the start of your next turn. You may choose to add this after seeing the roll. Upkeep: At the start of your next turn and every turn thereafter, you may spend 1 AP to continue this performance. Enemies that successfully made their save, do not have to save again against this continued performance, but those who were affected continue to be affected.

L4.name: Shatter
L4.description: Every enemy within range must make a save against your Potency or take Light Damage. Any unattended objects made of glass or a similar vulnerable material also shatter.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Art, Negotiation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus.

L8.name: Siren Song
L8.description: All enemies in range must make a save against your Potency. If they fail, they may only spend AP on their next turn to move closer to you using the most direct path possible and cannot make attacks before that. To move closer, they may take actions that would help in getting closer such as standing up from prone or using fae flash.  If this path leads them through an obviously harmful hazard such as off a cliff, they are no longer affected by this ability. Otherwise, this ability lasts until they are within 5ft of you or until they take damage. While playing this Song, you can take no other actions. Taking any other action cancels the Song, immediately freeing all enemies from its effects. A creature can only be affected by this ability once per encounter.

L1.name: Mage Armor
L1.description: You may use Mage Armor

L2.name: Dragon Breaks The Ranks
L2.description: You gain Flight and surround yourself in fire, then Sidestep to a point in a direct line. You destroy low cover you pass through, though the GM may rule that some cover is too sturdy to be destroyed. In either case, you continue until you reach the target and may make a single melee Heavy Attack against an enemy in range with your weapon. Any other characters in your path may spend 1 RP to move 5ft out of your charge or take Light Fire Damage. If you have an element mastered, you may change the Fire damage of this ability to the element you have mastered. This spell does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L3.name: Battle Cast
L3.description: The next Spell you cast before the end of your turn does not provoke an attack of opportunity from being casted in Melee Range of an opponent.

L4.name: Shooting Form
L4.description: You transform a melee weapon you’re holding into a Wand Channeling Weapon or return a transformed weapon back to normal. While transformed, the weapon keeps any relevant enchants and functions exactly like how a wand would. You are considered proficient in this wand, even if you normally wouldn’t be and can fire Arcane Elemental shots with it, even if you normally wouldn’t be able to. Lasts until the end of the encounter, for 5 minutes or until the weapon leaves your possession.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Intimidation, Magic, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus, Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Circle Swing
L8.description: You make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you may move the target to anywhere in your weapon range (Max 10ft) , then push it 5ft. The target must remain at the same height it was before this ability was used. Your next Spell or Channeling Weapon attack this turn costs 1 less AP (Min 0).

L1.name: Arms Mastery
L1.description: You are considered proficient with every melee weapon you wield (This does not count as being proficient for class requirements).

L2.name: Mordschlag
L2.description: You attack the target with the pommel or crossguard making a Heavy Attack against the target.

L3.name: Skillfull Blow
L3.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.

L4.name: Unconventional Weapon
L4.description: You may treat the weapon you’re currently wielding as a weapon of a different group for the next attack in addition to its normal group. In addition to receiving the base weapon properties of both weapon groups, this allows it to meet the requirements of abilities that require a certain group, and allows you to change its damage type to the chosen weapon’s damage type. The GM may rule that some abilities still cannot be performed with your equipped weapon.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Appraise, Athletics, Riding, Deception, Medicine, History, Perception, Insight, Survival, Negotiation, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Disarming Attack
L8.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you Disarm the target until the start of your next turn.

L1.name: Fallen Friend
L1.description: When an Heroic tier ally reaches 0 HP, you may immediately enter Rage Stance for 0 AP and RP as well as 0 mana. You can use this even if you don’t have the Rage ability yet.

L2.name: Rage Stance
L2.description: You enter a Stance in which you prioritize offense over defense. The Stance lasts until the encounter ends, you get Downed or up to an hour. While the Stance lasts, gain 2 additional AP that can only be used on melee attacks each turn (including the turn you use this), but cannot use actions that use RP other than attack of opportunity unless they have the Rage keyword. You also cannot use abilities with the Spell keyword, unless they also have the Rage keyword. You are treated as having 0 RP for abilities with that as a requirement. While in your Rage Stance, you gain a +1 bonus to Guard and a +5 bonus to all Fitness checks.

L3.name: Into the Fray
L3.description: You may move up to twice your movement speed. You must end this movement in range to make a melee attack against an enemy. Can only be used on your turn.

L4.name: Break Free
L4.description: You may use this ability when you would be affected by a Stun, Root, Fear or a similar effect that would prevent you from acting that's inflicted by an enemy. You may immediately enter Rage Stance without spending any AP or RP. You gain immunity to this effect for the duration of the Rage.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Perception, Survival, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Power or Focus.

L8.name: Reckless Strike
L8.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target.

L1.name: Transmuter of Life
L1.description: You may use Biohacking. You can store up to one gene in your gene inventory.

L2.name: Gene Activation
L2.description: You transmute yourself, consuming a gene. You transform,  granting yourself a single chimera racial ability that corresponds with the gene.  You are transformed until the end of your next turn. Doing this consumes the gene from your DNA. You can deactivate the transformation for 0 RP. You cannot obtain and use abilities that require an IP or EP to use through this ability.

L3.name: Helix Siphon
L3.description: When you make a melee attack against an enemy that is a Chimera, you copy some of their DNA, granting you a gene for any ability of their race. This gene is temporary and only lasts until the end of combat. Alternatively, you can touch an ally Chimera or downed enemy and copy a single gene.

L4.name: G. Shift
L4.description: You take 11 true damage, then choose to either lose 1 RP or reduce your AP by 1 for your first turn.  You temporarily mutate your body, granting an effect similar to one of the following Alchemy Elixirs:  Rabbit, Reaper, Shark, Tunnel, Bull, Bear. This effect does not stack with their corresponding Elixir Effect. This effect cannot be dispelled and lasts until the end of the encounter.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Riding, Appraise, Medicine, Magic, History, Animal Husbandry, Art or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Anima Synthesis Form
L8.description: You consume a gene and transform, granting yourself all abilities of the chimera subrace of that corresponding gene.  This lasts until the end of the encounter.  In addition, learning this skill grants you +1 to your gene inventory for the Biohacking skill. Despite being transformed, you can still use Gene Activation to add additional abilities. You cannot obtain and use abilities that require an IP or EP to use through this ability.

L1.name: Dark Surge
L1.description: After you deal damage to yourself, you gain Dark Surge for your next unarmed attack that turn. Dark Surge gives you +1 Accuracy.

L2.name: Inexorable Shadow Stance
L2.description: You enter a stance that enhances your attacks at the cost of your own life. Whenever you hit an enemy with an unarmed attack, you may deal Light or Heavy true damage to yourself to increase the damage of the attack by the same amount. This may be done after reactions to the attack have been declared, though an enemy may still choose to react to it again or differently afterwards. If used with an attack that hits more than 1 target, this extra damage only applies to one of the damage rolls. You may do this effect twice per round. If an enemy dodges to make you miss and refund the effect, it still counts as one of your uses. This stance lasts until you enter a different stance.

L3.name: Eclipse Breaker
L3.description: You make a Heavy attack against all targets in a 15x15ft line. You must choose the line direction when starting the ability.

L4.name: Focused Power
L4.description: When you’re about to take the extra damage from Inexorable Shadow Stance, you may choose to maximize the damage instead of rolling. Affects both the self damage and the damage you deal with it.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Deception, Intimidation, Insight, Medicine or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus, Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Shadow Counter
L8.description: You prepare a powerful heavy attack against a single enemy. This attack deals extra damage based on half your missing health. If that enemy provokes an AoO while you are charging this ability, you may immediately use this ability as if you finished charging it.

L1.name: Craft Weapons
L1.description: You gain Blacksmith Core Crafting Abilities. You can craft using the Iron and Tamahagane base materials, as well as any alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times. You can use a Smith’s Hammer as a crafting tool for blacksmithing crafts. You may also use it as a Bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient.

L2.name: Repair Weapon
L2.description: Errand. You repair a broken smithing weapon.

L3.name: Forged Knowledge
L3.description: Using your knowledge of weapons, you infuse the strongest point of your weapon with mana and then strike at the perfect angle dealing maximum weapon damage.  Maximize one damage dice for the attack.

L4.name: Steady Craft II
L4.description: You add a crafting check to the item. The crafting dice is automatically a 7.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Blacksmithing. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Riding or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power, Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Weapon Maintenance
L8.description: You recall having previously assisted an ally in maintaining their weapon, causing it to be in top shape for battle. This weapon gains a +1 Power Material Bonus or +1 Focus Material Bonus. If you smithed the weapon it gains both. This Artisan bonus is considered the same bonus as from a weapon's material like Tamahagane and does not stack with it. If the weapon you choose gets no bonus from this ability, it's critical range instead improves by 1. (A normal weapon now crits on a 19 and 20, a katana on a 18, 19 and 20).

L1.name: Transfusion
L1.description: You may use Transfusion.

L2.name: Crimson Enmity
L2.description: When your HP is at or below 50% of your max HP, you gain Crimson Enmity . When you would heal an ally or yourself with an ability from this class (Bloodbinder), you heal for 1.5x that amount.

L3.name: Blood Drain
L3.description: You make a Light Attack that deals Dark damage against the target. On damage, you recover HP equal to your Toughness + 3.

L4.name: Blood Shield
L4.description: You grant the target 1 RP that must be spent on the Block action against the triggering attack. They gain a +5 bonus to their Guard for this Block action. After the attack, the target regains HP equal to half the damage blocked (Blocked meaning the damage reduced through block, not necessarily your total block value).

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Medicine, Magic or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Life Sacrifice
L8.description: You lose HP until your HP reaches 1. Target ally within range recovers that much HP. Any HP that exceeds the target’s max HP becomes temporary HP. This HP loss cannot be mitigated in any way and ignores temporary HP. This does not trigger effects that would trigger upon taking damage.

L1.name: VIP Protection
L1.description: At the start of each arc, choose one person other than yourself as your VIP for that arc. Your VIP is the target for several of your abilities. You may use VIP Protection. If you choose a VIP, you cannot become the target of a VIP protection yourself.

L2.name: Shelter
L2.description: The attack targeting your VIP instead targets you. You may use this even if you’re also a target of the attack and both attacks will resolve against you. You also gain the benefits of the Block action against the triggering attack.

L3.name: Temporary Protection
L3.description: Until the end of the encounter, the target ally you selected is also treated as your VIP.

L4.name: Vengeance
L4.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. If your VIP is at 0 HP or lower, this attack gains Lock On and Guard Crush.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Toughness.

L8.name: At your side
L8.description: You teleport up to 40ft. This must place you next to your VIP.

L1.name: Botany Study
L1.description: You gain the ability to detect if foraged food is poisonous or unsafe to eat, nearly guaranteeing its safety.  If it is a common poison, you can also identify its effect. You gain the ability to properly process some poisonous mushrooms, fruits and herbs, making them edible as a Camp Action.  You can process up to 100u at a time. You gain the ability to harvest Tier 2 Foraging Outcrops.

L2.name: Memory of the Grove
L2.description: After making a gathering check, you may choose to reroll the die. If you do, store the original die result. You may replace a future gathering die result (before bonus) with the stored value.

L3.name: Trail Mix
L3.description: For a camp action you create up to 5 servings of a trail meal.  It costs 20u of food per serving and only gives 1 mana (0 for Mirane), but it keeps starvation away.  Does not require a culinary kit, and does not count as cooking for the purposes of culinary actions such as using stocks.

L4.name: Stalks of Comparison
L4.description: Make a gathering check and apply it to either Node Points or Lucky Points. If the result of this check (before bonuses) is higher than your previous roll this session, gain a bonus to the result equal to your Foraging skill (max +10).

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Foraging.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Reason or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Toughness or Agility.

L8.name: Forager's Eye
L8.description: Initiate a sight and sound based search for hidden plants, fungi, creatures or living enemy units in a 30x30 area within range.  You become automatically aware of the targeted species of plants, fungi or creatures or you can choose to be aware of all hostile enemy units.  You must have line of sight to the target in order to spot them.  After revealing the target your Lifebeat Sense gains (if used immediately after): Combo:  If used after Forager's Eye, the AP and mana cost of this is reduced by 1 and the target's location is known to you and your allies until the end of your next turn. This removes Hiding from them.

L1.name: Shrug it off
L1.description: You may use Vital Drive II.

L2.name: Counter Stance
L2.description: You enter a stance in which you freely counterattack against enemies that attack you. After an attack against you has resolved, you may make a Melee Attack of Opportunity against that target. While in this stance, you cannot use the Dodge or Block reactions. You may spend 0 AP or 1 RP to cancel this stance. It otherwise lasts until you enter another stance.

L3.name: Wind-up Punch
L3.description: You may make a Heavy attack against the target. Overcharge: You may spend additional AP when using this ability to deal an additional Light Damage per AP used. Add all of this damage together before applying damage reduction from Guard or abilities.

L4.name: Suckerpunch
L4.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Unbalanced.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Insight, Survival, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Knockout Punch
L8.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On damage if the enemy is a Grunt, they immediately drop to 0 HP and fall unconscious.

L1.name: Carpenter Basics
L1.description: You can use a carpenters chisel as a crafting tool for carpenters crafts and buildings. You can craft using Pine and Maple, as well as alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times. You gain proficiency in axes. You also gain proficiency in either bows or crossbows.

L2.name: Repair Wood Weapon
L2.description: Errand. You repair a broken carpentry weapon.

L3.name: Hammer and Nail
L3.description: Make a light attack against the target with Sure Hit and Full Pierce.

L4.name: In The Zone
L4.description: You add a crafting check to the item. This craft gains a +X bonus where X is equal to the amount of crafting dice spent this session.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Carpentry. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Athletics, History or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power, Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Felling Strike
L8.description: Make a heavy attack against a target enemy that is no larger than large size category. On damage, sidestep to any unoccupied space next to the target, of the same elevation as the target, and knockback the target 10ft.

L1.name: Mounted Basics
L1.description: You gain proficiency in Polearms, Lances, Light armor, Shields and one other common weapon group of your choice. You gain 5 skill points in Riding. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills. You may use Spirit Jump.

L2.name: Canter
L2.description: You may Sidestep 10ft.

L3.name: Horse Whistle
L3.description: Your mount (the last mount you were riding on this arc) may sidestep up to its speed towards you and it gains Trample (as per the ability, if you have it) for this movement. If this movement moves your mount onto your square, you may mount it for 0 RP. If you were prone and mount your mount, you are no longer prone.

L4.name: Rider & Mount As One
L4.description: While you are mounted and not flying you and your mount are considered the same entity for the purposes of what can target you. This means that while you are mounted, your mount cannot be targeted by anything (including allied effects) unless the effect specifically says it does. Any debuff or buff on you also affects your mount's movement and speed as if it was placed on the mount.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Animal Husbandry, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Trample
L8.description: You may activate this after moving at least 20ft in a movement action to gain Trample until you stop moving. Abilities with the Teleport keyword do not apply for this, but declaring multiple AP to move at the same time counts as 1 action. While under the effects of trample, you don't provoke attacks of opportunity by moving and you can move into enemies that are at least one size smaller than your mount. An enemy may spend 1 RP to move out of the way to an empty spot within 5ft of them. If they don’t move out of the way, you make a Sure Hit Light attack against them with your mount, using your mount’s stats. On damage, you knock them prone into the square you came from and keep moving, occupying the square they were in. Otherwise your movement action ends. Each enemy can only be affected by trample once per activation of the ability, regardless of whether they are attacked or move out of it.

L1.name: Past Self
L1.description: You may use Past Self. Using the Delay encounter start action does not consume your encounter start action use and may use it multiple times.

L2.name: Accelerating Strike
L2.description: You make a Heavy Attack against the target. Afterwards, you gain Accelerating Strike , which increases your Initiative by up to 2.

L3.name: Delayed Hit
L3.description: You make a Heavy Attack against the target, but don’t deal damage. On hit, even if it was guarded, the target takes Heavy Damage at the start of your next turn. (This delayed damage is calculated of the target’s Guard at the time it’s applied, not when the attack hit)

L4.name: Attacks from the Past
L4.description: After making a melee weapon attack, you may repeat the attack from one of your past self tokens. You may target the same enemy as your original attack. Cannot repeat Secret Arts or other attacks with a once per combat/arc etc type limitation. Can only repeat attacks with a base cost of 2 AP or lower and any mana cost must be paid as normal.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception, Magic or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: Everything, Everywhere, All At Once
L8.description: You make a Heavy Attack against the enemy.  You may then repeat this attack from each of your past selves and may target the same enemy multiple times.

L1.name: Store Time
L1.description: You can use Store Time.

L2.name: Slow
L2.description: The target is slowed until the start of your next turn unless the target pays 1 RP to resist this effect.

L3.name: Accelerando
L3.description: Grant yourself or an ally +4 initiative.

L4.name: Haste
L4.description: Grants the target Haste, granting them 2 AP for the rest of this turn.  A target can only be affected by haste once per turn.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Magic or History. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason or Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Agility.

L8.name: Secret Art: Galaxy Stop
L8.description: You create a space-time anomaly that freezes time itself in a local area for a short while, affecting all but the strong-willed.  All non-heroic units in range cannot take any actions until the end of their next turn.

L1.name: Titan's Grip
L1.description: You may wield a Two-Handed weapon in 1 hand allowing you to use a second (Two-Handed) weapon or shield in your offhand.  While dual wielding heavy weapons, you may make dual wield attacks as if they were light weapons.

L2.name: Mighty Leap
L2.description: You jump to a target within range and may make a Light attack against them. All enemies within 5ft of the target must make a save or become  Unbalanced .

L3.name: Double Slam
L3.description: You may make up to 2 Light attacks against the target, paying 1 AP and 1 Mana for each. On damage of either hit, the target becomes  Unbalanced . If the target is already unbalanced, they are knocked prone. If you are wielding only 1 Two-Handed weapon, you are limited to 1 attack.

L4.name: Two Sword Crush
L4.description: You may add your Light damage to the attack.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: Titan Blade
L8.description: You greatly increase the size and weight of your weapons, then slam them down in a direction. You make a melee Heavy attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide line up to the range. On damage, a target is knocked prone.   After the initial hit, a shockwave follows. You make another Heavy attack against any enemy still in the original line’s range. This second attack has the Sure Hit and Full Pierce qualities against a prone target.

L1.name: Ice Cold
L1.description: You are immune to Slows and difficult terrain effects with the Frost keyword. You are immune to regular weather effects involving cold. You gain Frost Mastery.  Mastery of an element allows you to use it in Channeling weapon attacks and use it to perform minor tricks relating to it.

L2.name: Icicle Nail
L2.description: You fire a shard of ice at a target unit, making a Light Attack that deals Frost Damage. On hit, the target is Slowed until the end of their next turn.

L3.name: Sheet Ice
L3.description: You freeze the ground in a 20x20 area, creating difficult terrain that lasts until the end of your next turn. You may use 1 mana per instance of this spell to continue the effect until the end of your next turn.

L4.name: Glacial Wall
L4.description: You create 4 * X connected ice structures that are 5ft x 5ft cubes that last for up to 24 hours. These cubes have HP equal to 20 + Focus. These structures can function as low cover. Ice Wall segments take double damage from Fire Attacks.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to your Focus, Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Frost Coffin
L8.description: You cast a Spell that flash freezes a target Grunt, killing them instantly and creates a two Glacial Wall cube stack at their location. These cubes have HP equal to 15 + Focus. These structures can function as cover. Ice wall segments take double damage from Fire Attacks.

L1.name: Culinary Basics
L1.description: You gain Culinarian Core Crafting Abilities You can craft stocks using advanced and base materials. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times. You can use a Frying Pan as a crafting tool for culinarian crafts. You may also use it as a Light Bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient.

L2.name: Perfect Seasoning
L2.description: Make a crafting check with a +3 Bonus.

L3.name: Oil Slick
L3.description: You throw a bottle of oil on the ground within range, creating a 15x15 area of slippery oil. If the target contacts the oil at any point of movement (even the start or end) and they moved more than 5ft in a single movement action, they immediately fall prone. The slick lasts until the start of your next turn.

L4.name: Craft Pemmican
L4.description: Errand. You turn up to 1000u of food into 25 Pemmican Bars (40u of food per bar), which are incredibly shelf stable and easy to eat.  They perform about the same as Military Rations but they are wrapped by special leaves that deter pests—useful and consistent adventure food. You may also use this as an IP action to convert up to 2000u of food into 50 Pemmican bars.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Culinary. You also gain 5 skill points to spend in Art, Medicine, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Pepper Spray
L8.description: You reach into your pocket and pull out a handful of ground chili pepper that you throw in front of you in a 15ft cube. Inflicts all targets with  Pepper Spray , causing Blinded 4 and the inability to smell.  The target or an ally of the target within 5ft can spend 1 AP to wipe it off. Otherwise the effect ends at the end of the target’s next turn. You may spend 1 RP and 1 additional mana instead of paying the AP cost.

L1.name: Cultivator's Journey
L1.description: Your unarmed attack damage is treated as a One-Handed Weapon in which you are proficient. You gain proficiency in light armor, light swords and another weapon of your choice. You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Athletics, Deception, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation, Stealth or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L2.name: Ki Circulation
L2.description: You cycle the Ki inside your body and purge it from negative energy. You remove Bleeding, Shaken, Unbalanced or any Poison effect from yourself.

L3.name: Ki Sense
L3.description: You send out your Ki and become aware of any creatures without presence concealment within 40ft of you, giving you a rough idea of their location. This does not remove Hiding from them, but their attacks against you do not gain Trick Attack or any other stealth or surprise bonuses until the start of your next turn.

L4.name: Ki Recovery
L4.description: You spend your Encounter Conclusion action to regain 2 mana.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Athletics, Deception, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation, Stealth or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Sword Riding Art
L8.description: You jump onto a light sword, versatile melee weapon, greatshield, bayonet musket or two-handed melee weapon you’re carrying and use it to move around. While riding on top of the sword, you gain Flight. You can fly this way for up to 5 minutes. If you take damage while flying higher than 5ft above the ground, your altitude drops by 20ft. If this would reduce your altitude to the point where you touch the ground, you may hop off your weapon and put it back into your hand if possible for 0 RP. After this happens, your Sword Riding ends. If the attack that reduced your HP came from a flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude but instead become unbalanced. If you are knocked back by an effect, you must make a save against the attacker's potency. Else, you are blown off your sword.

L1.name: On my terms
L1.description: While you are at 1 HP or higher, damage (including true damage) you take during your own turn cannot reduce you below 1 HP. When you roll a 1 on a skill check, something unexpected may happen (GM's discretion). This is usually bad, but it could also turn out to be a blessing in disguise.

L2.name: Backlash
L2.description: You make a Light attack against the target. You take true damage equal to half the damage dealt.

L3.name: Devil's Curse
L3.description: The attacker takes damage equal to the damage roll of the triggering attack. They cannot Dodge or Block this damage. Does not work against Secret Arts, Climax Arts or similar level abilities.

L4.name: Devil's Blow
L4.description: You make a Heavy attack against the target. This attack does NOT have Full Pierce. If you roll a 1 on the accuracy check, you take full pierce damage of same the type as the attack equal to the damage roll of the attack. You also regain the mana spent on this.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Appraise, Athletics, Deception, Perception, Insight, Negotiation, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
