In addition, any abilities with the Aid keyword have their range increased by 20ft while inside the Aura.

L4.name: Divine Smite II
L4.description: You may make a Heavy Attack that deals Holy Damage against the target. On damage, you may give yourself or an ally within 30ft temporary HP equal your 5+Power until the end of the encounter.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Lay on Hands
L8.description: You Heal the target for 4 + Power. Overcharge: You may spend an additional mana to remove a negative effect from the target. Injuries and Mortal Wounds cannot be removed with this.

L1.name: Soul Bond
L1.description: When you acquire this ability, choose 1 willing person. That person is treated as your Soulmate and several of the class abilities only work on your Soulmate. This cannot be changed unless your Soulmate dies. Your Soulmate is always treated as your VIP for any bodyguard abilities. You may still designate another VIP as normal.

L2.name: Lifesense
L2.description: You know the direction your Soulmate is in and if you’re within 300 feet of them, you know their exact location. You also know their current HP, Mana and RP.

L3.name: Faithful Shield
L3.description: You may use Shelter once at 0 RP cost if the target is your Soulmate. This effect lasts until the start of your next turn. Overcharge: You may spend 4 AP on this ability to instead gain 2 free uses of Shelter. These free uses ignore the normal limitation of using an ability only once per turn.

L4.name: Quick Assist
L4.description: You teleport next to your soulmate and force the triggering attack to resolve against you, gaining the benefit of the Block action against it. Afterwards, if your Soulmate is in range of the attacker, they may make an Attack of Opportunity against it. If you were already next to your Soulmate before you used this ability, the mana cost is reduced by 1.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception, Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Dynamic Duo
L8.description: You challenge all enemies in range until the start of your next turn. Any time you would be allowed to make an Attack of Opportunity, your Soulmate may make it instead. They may Sidestep up to their speed before making each Attack of Opportunity.

L1.name: Recover Power
L1.description: Any temporary HP on you at the end of an encounter is converted into healing. You regain HP equal to half the value of temporary HP you have remaining.

L2.name: Power Word: Shield
L2.description: The target gains temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to 11 + (2 x Power).

L3.name: Power Word: Barrier
L3.description: Up to 5 target allies within range (including yourself) gain temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to 10 + Power. You may pay either the AP or RP cost for this ability, you don't need to pay both.

L4.name: Power Word: Reinforce
L4.description: The target gains temporary HP equal to 2 x their current temporary HP.  After the attack is done resolving, the target's temporary HP is set to the value it was before this ability was used unless the target's current temporary HP is lower than that.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Magic, Religion, Medicine, Perception, Negotiation or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Lifesaver
L8.description: The target gains temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to their missing HP.

L1.name: Death Perception
L1.description: You may use Death Perception. One of your eyes becomes a Shinigami Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.

L2.name: True Death
L2.description: Damage from melee weapon attacks made by you also reduces the target’s max HP by the same amount. This lasts until the end of the encounter.

L3.name: Line of Death
L3.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. Overcharge: You may spend 1 additional mana and AP to make another Light Attack against the same target.

L4.name: Point of Death
L4.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. This attack deals maximum damage instead of rolling for the damage.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Stealth, Medicine, Perception, Insight or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: Seventeen Dissection
L8.description: You deal 17 true damage to the target. If this damage lowers the target’s HP to 0 or lower, they immediately die. If the target of this attack is a non-heroic unit, they instantly die regardless of their remaining HP.

L1.name: Air Stability
L1.description: The cavalier’s Rider & Mount As One ability applies even while flying. Any damage you take while flying on a flying mount will cause you to lose altitude as if your mount took damage.

L2.name: Aerial Crashdown
L2.description: You and your mount crash down on the target. You move towards the target and your mount makes a Heavy attack against it. On damage, the target is knocked prone.

L3.name: Aerial Loop
L3.description: You and your mount take the Dodge action and gain a +5 bonus to evasion against the attack.

L4.name: Flyby Attack
L4.description: You sidestep up to your speed. At any point during this movement, you may spend 1 AP to make a Light attack with a weapon or spend 2 AP to make a Heavy attack with a weapon. After this attack, you may change the direction in which you spend the rest of your movement.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Animal Husbandry, Riding, Flight, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power

L8.name: Secret Art: Mythical Awakening
L8.description: You channel your power into your mount, allowing it to unleash a devastating attack based on what type of mount it is. Pegasus: Bellerophon: You Sidestep towards a target within 60ft and make a Heavy attack against it. This attack has Lock-on and costs 1 RP and 2 Mana Wyvern: Reginliev: You make a Heavy attack against all enemies in a 5ft wide line up to a point within 60ft. You also make a Heavy attack against all enemies within 10ft of the selected point, though enemies can also take an attack from this ability once. This attack costs 2 AP and 2 Mana. Dragonfly: Darting Mirage: You move at incredible speed and create several illusionary afterimages in your wake. Until the start of your next turn, every attack against you or your mount that is not a critical hit or does not have the Sure Hit keyword  automatically misses. This is an Illusion effect. This ability costs 0 AP and 4 Mana. All these attacks are made with your mount’s stats instead of your own.

L1.name: Boundless Wind
L1.description: You may use Boundless Wind Stance.

L2.name: Master of the Empty Sky
L2.description: When flying, you gain a +2 Cover bonus to your Evasion and Dodge against ranged attacks.

L3.name: Heavenfall Descent
L3.description: You crash to the ground without taking falling damage and make a Light attack against all enemies within 5ft of you. May target a point on the floor as long as it’s within 20ft of you horizontally.

L4.name: Swift Wind Palm
L4.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target is knocked down 30ft or until they hit the floor (on top of any other height loss from being hit while flying) and you may Sidestep next to them afterwards.  A target knocked down on to the ground with this ability is knocked prone and takes falling damage as normal. They do not take the normal extra damage as result of being knocked into the floor.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Flight, Perception, Intimidation, Survival or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Agility.

L8.name: Cloud Piercing Claw
L8.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, they are knocked airborne 20ft until the end of your turn (if this attack is made outside of your turn, they drop immediately). They are NOT knocked prone from this, but do take fall damage like normal. You may Sidestep up to 25ft into the air after making this attack, regardless of if you did damage with it. Combo : If used after entering Boundless Wind Stance, the mana cost of this ability is reduced by 1.

L1.name: Snipe
L1.description: You gain proficiency in Sniper Rifles. If you did not move this turn or the previous turn you gain a +1d4 Snipe bonus to your accuracy roll on attacks made with a Sniper Rifle.

L2.name: Penetrating Shot
L2.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. This attack penetrates through thin layers of cover such as wood planks (GM’s decision), negating the bonus it would give.

L3.name: Concealed Shot
L3.description: You immediately make a new stealth check and remain hidden if enemies fail their perception check.

L4.name: Aimed Shot
L4.description: You make a Heavy Attack against the target.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain 5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Stealth, Medicine, Perception, Survival, Animal Husbandry or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Cunning or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Killshot
L8.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target that deals double Heavy Damage instead. If fired in the first round of combat while all enemies are unaware of you, the target has to spend an additional RP to react to your attack. This stacks with the Trick Attack keyword, but is ineffective against abilities that negate surprise or stealth attacks such as Instinct.

L1.name: Sorcerer's Journey
L1.description: You gain proficiency in Channeling Weapons and Light Armor. You gain +5 skill points in Magic. You gain Arcane Mastery. Upon mastering this class, you may master an additional element of your choice.

L2.name: Magic Blast
L2.description: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Arcane damage. If you have an element mastered, you may change the damage of this spell to that element.

L3.name: Arcane Rapidfire
L3.description: You make 4 light attacks that deal Arcane damage against the target.

L4.name: Arcane Blast
L4.description: You make a Heavy attack that deals Arcane damage against the target.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Deception, Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Negotiation, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Circuit Reset
L8.description: You reset your circuits, allowing you to once again benefit from the Circuit effect.

L1.name: Versatile Range
L1.description: You may treat your polearm’s threatened range as 5ft instead of 5-10ft. You must declare this during your turn and it lasts until the start of your next turn.

L2.name: Draw-In
L2.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, they are pulled up to 5ft towards you. Combo: If used after Lunge, the range of this attack and the pullback distance are doubled. This consumes the Lunge effect.

L3.name: Wild Thrusts
L3.description: You rapidly thrust your weapon in front of you, making a Light attack against all enemies in a 15ft wide, 10ft long box in front of you. Until the start of your next turn, you may make Attacks of Opportunity against any enemy walking through that box. You cannot make any other Attacks of Opportunity while this effect lasts. You may cancel this effect for 0 RP.

L4.name: Spear Charge
L4.description: You may spend 2 or 4 AP on this ability and move as though you had spent that AP on movement. After you finish moving, you may spend half of the AP you used on this ability on a single Light or Heavy attack. For every 10ft moved, this attack gains +1 damage up to a maximum of +10. If you spent 4 AP on this ability, you may also choose to use Wild Thrusts instead of making a Light or Heavy attack. These attacks only gain the damage bonus listed above for the initial attacks (and not the constant attacks of opportunity) and Wild Thrusts mana cost is reduced to 0 if used in this way.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Riding, Intimidation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: Gae Bolg
L8.description: Make a Heavy attack against the target. Unless your accuracy roll is a 1, your roll is treated as if you rolled a 20 and it gains Sure Hit.  It is treated as a critical hit even though it's Sure Hit, unless the target blocks.

L1.name: Draw Elements II
L1.description: You may use Draw Elements II.

L2.name: Spellblade's Preparation
L2.description: You unsheathe your weapon and enchant your weapon with an element you've mastered. Your weapon now deals the chosen element’s damage instead of its normal damage. If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit. The weapon gains a +1 Power bonus to attacks until the end of the encounter.

L3.name: Elemental Sweep
L3.description: You may make a Light Attack against all enemies within range. On damage, your attack applies an additional effect based on one of your attack's damage type. (If you are able to have multiple damage types on the attack such as through Holy weapon, you may pick any of the effects). Arcane: The target loses 1 mana. Fire: The target becomes Burning X, where X equals your Power x 3. Water: The target becomes Soaked X, where X equals your power/2 (rounded down), until they take Lightning damage. Lightning: The target is afflicted with Static X, where X equals your Power x 3.  Earth/Frost: The target becomes Slowed until the start of your next turn. Wind: You may push the target back 10ft. Holy: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Power+5. Dark: The target becomes Blinded 5 until after their next attack.

L4.name: Double Elements
L4.description: Your next Elemental Strike or Elemental Sweep ability used this turn may add in the effect of another element. This element must be one you have mastered and different from the effect that's already dealt by the attack.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Intimidation, Magic, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: Spectrum Blade
L8.description: Until the end of the encounter, you enchant your weapon with every single element. When using Elemental Strike or Elemental Sweep, you may choose which effect you want regardless of the damage type your attack deals. When you deal damage, you apply the damage type that would deal the most damage. In addition, your weapon gains a +1 Power bonus until the end of the encounter. If it already had a +1 Power bonus through an enchantment, this bonus is instead increased to +2. If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.

L1.name: Draw Elements
L1.description: You may use Draw Elements.

L2.name: Imbue Weapon
L2.description: You enchant your melee weapon with an element you mastered. Your weapon now deals the chosen element’s damage instead of its normal damage. If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.

L3.name: Elemental Strike
L3.description: You make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, your attack applies an additional effect based on one of your attack's damage type. (If you are able to have multiple damage types on the attack such as through Holy weapon, you may pick any of the effects). Arcane: The target loses 1 mana.  Fire: The target becomes Burning X, where X equals your Power x 3.  Water: The target becomes Soaked X, where X equals your power/2 (rounded down), until they take Lightning damage. Lightning: The target is afflicted with Static X, where X equals your Power x 3 Earth/Frost: The target becomes Slowed until the start of your next turn. Wind: You may push the target back 10ft.  Holy: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Power + 5. Dark: The target becomes Blinded 5 until after their next attack.

L4.name: Spellstrike
L4.description: You choose an ability with the Spell keyword that you can use. You make a melee attack against a target in range for the same AP and mana as the Spell.  Your attack functions like the chosen Spell does, but does not provoke an Attack of Opportunity and is not treated as casting a Spell (meaning it can be used while Raging and Anti-Spell effects don't work). If the chosen spell was an AoE effect, the target point would be the target you strike with the spell. The requirements for applying any effects remain the same.  The spell you chose cannot be used again in the same round like normal, and if used with Secret Arts it counts as your Secret Art use for that encounter. Only works with Spells that are attacks.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Athletics, Intimidation, Magic, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Greater Imbue Weapon
L8.description: You may enchant your weapon as though you had used Imbue Weapon, but it also gains a +1 Power bonus in addition to the normal benefits. If you are not holding the enchanted weapon at the start of your turn, your weapon loses this benefit.

L1.name: Celestial Artillery
L1.description: When attacking an enemy that’s further than 80ft away, your spells with the Celestial keyword gain the Artillery keyword. You may use Comet.

L2.name: Starfall
L2.description: You target a 25x25 square within range. On the start of your next turn, 4 stars fall down in the area. At the start of your next turn, you make 4 light attacks that deal Arcane damage, evenly divided amongst enemies in the area. If there are less enemies than stars, you may hit an enemy more than once (though you must still attempt to hit each enemy an equal amount of times if possible).

L3.name: Corona Burst
L3.description: You make a Heavy attack against the target that deals Fire damage. For every 20ft of distance the target is away from you, this deals +1 damage. (Up to a maximum of +12)

L4.name: Stardust Reverie
L4.description: Declare a distance up to the range of this spell. You create a celestial dome at that range from you that harm those that pass through it. Until the start of your next turn, whenever a creature willingly enters that distance from you, you may make a Light attack against them that deals Arcane damage. You may choose to spend an RP before making this attack to make it a Heavy attack. A character that Sidesteps or Teleports through your stars does not provoke this attack. Upkeep : 1 mana. When you pay the upkeep cost, you may change the distance of the stars.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Appraise, Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Starlance
L8.description: You make a Precise attack against the target that deals Arcane damage.

L1.name: Shielding Fist
L1.description: While you have at least 1 hand empty and aren't wielding a shield, you gain a +4 Shield bonus to your block.

L2.name: Steadfast Earth Stance
L2.description: You enter a stance that grants you the toughness of the earth. You gain a +2 bonus to your guard and a +6 bonus to your Block. This stance lasts until you enter a different stance.

L3.name: Hardening Fist
L3.description: You make a Light attack against the target. Afterwards, you gain Hardening Fist , which gives +2 guard and +8 Block, but lose 4 evasion (including dodge) until the start of your next turn. Combo : If used after Steadfast Earth Stance, the mana cost is reduced by 1.

L4.name: Earthen Guardian
L4.description: You jump and sidestep towards the attacked ally and the triggering attack resolves against you instead. You automatically take the Block action against this attack.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Jade Skin
L8.description: You take the Block action against the triggering attack. If you reduce the damage to 0, the attacker becomes Disarmed until the start of their next turn.

L1.name: Sound the retreat
L1.description: You may use Sound the retreat

L2.name: Wall Formation
L2.description: Until the start of your next turn, every ally within range gains Wall Formation . If they are adjacent to another ally gains a +1 bonus to their guard and a +4 bonus to their block value.

L3.name: Feigned Retreat
L3.description: All allies in a 15x15ft square within range may Sidestep up to their speed towards you.  If they successfully left an enemy’s melee range and are not in an enemy’s melee range after moving, then the first time an enemy enters their melee range before the start of their next turn, they may make an Attack of Opportunity against that enemy.

L4.name: Anvil Tactics
L4.description: A single ally in range may move up to their speed. If they end up in melee range of an enemy and there is an ally on the direct opposite side of that enemy, they may make an Attack of Opportunity. This attack gains Lock On.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Deception, Stealth, Appraise, History, Magic, Perception, Intimidation, Negotiation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason, Awareness or Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Agility.

L8.name: Secret Art: Shock & Awe
L8.description: You and up to 3 other allies within range gain 3 AP they must spend immediately. On their next turn, they regain 1 less AP. These AP may not be spent on Setup abilities and normal once per turn ability limitations apply.

L1.name: Summoning Basics
L1.description: You may channel for 10 minutes after which you arrive at the space between worlds, allowing you to interact with Eidolons that wish to meet with you. You may use Attune Eidolon.

L2.name: Channel Eidolon
L2.description: You use the ability granted by your attuned Eidolon. All costs and effects are determined by your attunement.

L3.name: Summon Lesser Eidolon
L3.description: Your contracted Eidolon hears your call and sends their minions to fight for you. The summon called and costs are dependent on your attunement. Lasts up to 5 minutes. You must pay the upkeep cost under the Summon’s  Summon Skill or the Summon vanishes. Unless the summon has a skill that says otherwise, a summon acts after you on initiative, but cannot act the round it is summoned except for RP actions.

L4.name: Unsummon
L4.description: Unsummon your Eidolon or Lesser Eidolon.  Recover mana equal to half of the mana cost of the summon cost. You may use this as an Encounter Conclusion action.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non-crafting and non-gathering skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Summon Eidolon
L8.description: Your contracted Eidolon hears your call and sends a proxy of themselves to fight for you.  The Eidolon called and the costs are dependent on your attunement.  Lasts up to 5 minutes.  You must pay the upkeep cost under the Eidolon’s  Summon Skill or the Eidolon vanishes. Unless the summon has a skill that says otherwise, a summon acts after you on initiative, but cannot act the round it is summoned, except for RP actions. Overcharge : You may spend 5 mana to give your summon the ability to act on the turn it is summoned.

L1.name: Take the advantage
L1.description: When attacking a target inflicted with Offstep, Stagger or Blinded, you gain a +1 accuracy bonus against them.

L2.name: Staggering Slice
L2.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, you inflict Stagger 1 until the end of your next turn.. If you're under the effects of Exposing Edge, you inflict Stagger 2 on damage instead.

L3.name: Deckbreaker
L3.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, you inflict Offstep on the target for the next attack against it. If the target is inflicted with Stagger, the mana cost of this is reduced by 1. If you're under the effects of Exposing Edge, this attack gains Lock On.

L4.name: Dust Kick
L4.description: You kick the ground in front of you, causing a veil of dust to appear. The target becomes Blinded 3 until the attack resolves. If the triggering attack misses, you kick the target and may push it 5ft into any horizontal direction. If under the effects of Exposing Edge, you apply Blinded 5 instead.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Stealth, Athletics, Roguecraft, Survival or  Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Exposing Edge
L8.description: You sharpen your blades, enhancing the effects of your debuffs. Upkeep: You may spend 1 AP or 1 mana to continue the effect.

L1.name: Holy Weapon
L1.description: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Holy Damage instead of its normal damage type. You may choose to have your weapon emit light as if it was a lantern. You can enable or disable this effect for 1 AP.

L2.name: Mark of Justice
L2.description: You mark a single enemy, inflicting Mark of Justice on them. A marked enemy’s movement speed is reduced to 20ft. Abilities that allow for movement cannot move the target for more than 20ft per use. Secret Arts and Esoteric Arts are not affected by this. You can only have a single enemy marked by this at the same time. It lasts until the end of the encounter. Does not work on vehicles. A marked target may spend 1 mana per AP/RP of the action (with a minimum of 1 mana even for 0 AP abilities) to temporarily ignore the restricting effect for a movement action.

L3.name: Justice Blade
L3.description: You make a Heavy Attack against the target.

L4.name: Paragon of Virtue
L4.description: You may remove a single negative non-physical effect from yourself (ie, fears, charms but not things such as bleed). If you are afflicted with more than 1 negative effect that is a valid target, you may choose which one you remove.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Divine Smite
L8.description: You may make a Heavy Attack that deals Holy Damage against the target. If your target’s HP is lower than the max damage your attack would deal, this attack gains Full Pierce and does maximum damage instead of rolling. This calculation only involves your base damage (Base weapon damage + the power). Any other effects added to the damage such as Heavy Blade's bonus damage, Anathema on target etc do not affect the calculation and aren't maximized.

L1.name: Resheathe
L1.description: You may use Resheathe.

L2.name: Kiri-tsuke II
L2.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. On Killing Blow, you regain the Iai state. Combo: If used after Iai Strike, the AP cost is reduced by 1.

L3.name: Flash Cut
L3.description: You draw your katana and attack with such Speed it becomes impossible to perceive, making a Heavy Attack against the target, after which you resheathe the weapon. This ability does not break the Iai state.

L4.name: Chiburi Noto II
L4.description: You clean the blood off your katana and sheath the blade. If your next Iai strike with this weapon during this arc is a hit, its Roll Damage is maximized and it gains Full Pierce.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Intimidation, History, Insight, Art, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Awareness or Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: Tsubame Gaeshi
L8.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target 3 times. On the first miss, the next attacks gain Lock On. On the second miss, the next attack gains Sure Hit. These only apply to attacks made during Tsubame Gaeshi.

L1.name: Scholar of Tactics
L1.description: You gain proficiency in Pistols. You may use Take Cover!.

L2.name: Tag Rotation
L2.description: The target willing allies, or yourself and a target willing ally switch places. This movement does not trigger any attacks of opportunity. You cannot use this if it causes an ally unit to occupy an invalid space. You may use either AP or RP for this ability.

L3.name: Order: Retreat
L3.description: You order one willing allied unit to retreat, moving up to their Speed. All movement must be made towards you, and does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

L4.name: Stratagem: Lightning
L4.description: All allied units (including yourself) gain +1 to Initiative and may immediately move up to their Speed.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on History.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in this skill.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason, Awareness or Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Agility.

L8.name: Attack in Detail
L8.description: You target one enemy unit. All allied units (including yourself) gain a +1 Damage Bonus when that enemy unit is the target of an attack until the start of your next turn. In addition, all allied units gain +5 Movement Speed when moving towards that enemy unit.

L1.name: Improvised Weaponry
L1.description: When using improvised weapons, they deal the same damage as normal weapons would.  You may use Chair Grab. Upon mastering this class, your improvised weapon attacks all gain the Prone keyword.

L2.name: Bottle Smash
L2.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Dazed. After this attack, your improvised weapon breaks.

L3.name: Improvised Defense
L3.description: You grab a nearby item and use it as cover against the triggering attack while taking the Dodge or Block action. The type of item used determines whether it is light, heavy or full cover.

L4.name: Bottle Toss
L4.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Dazed. After this attack, your improvised weapon breaks.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Insight, Survival, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Toughness.

L8.name: Tackle
L8.description: You attempt to tackle the target and may make a Heavy Attack against them. This attack deals no damage. On hit and if the target did not use the Block action against this ability, you and the target fall Prone. If the target successfully dodges this ability, you fall Prone.

L1.name: Mending
L1.description: You may spend an EP to restore a Broken item. Some items (such as consumables) cannot be repaired (GM’s discretion).

L2.name: Position Rewind
L2.description: You teleport to where you were at the start of your last turn. (Use a token to mark the position)

L3.name: Stasis
L3.description: You put a Grunt level character or any willing ally into Stasis until the start of your next turn. While in Stasis, they cannot be affected by any ability, attack or anything similar but they also cannot take any actions.

L4.name: Delay Time
L4.description: You choose one or more negative effects on the target that would trigger at the start or end of their turn. They trigger on the turn after the turn they would normally trigger instead.  You may only delay an effect once, but you are able to delay Stasis even though the target is normally immune to all abilities. Start of turn effects still trigger at the start of the turn and end of turn effects still trigger at the end of the turn.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Cunning or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Double Accel
L8.description: You gain 4 AP. Your AP recovery is lowered by 3 until the end of your next turn.

L1.name: Infiltrate
L1.description: When you use Decipher Magic, you may use Roguecraft instead of Magic as the skill for it. In addition, you can modify the effect in such a way that it no longer affects you and your allies rather than completely disabling it.

L2.name: Fast Hands
L2.description: You reduce the AP cost of your next Interact with object action this turn by 1.

L3.name: Snatch
L3.description: You may use this when a target in range uses the interact with object action. If you can hold the object the target is interacting with in one hand, you steal it from them. Any AP they may have spent on their action is not refunded to them.

L4.name: Quick Getaway
L4.description: You may move as if you had spent 2 AP on the movement action. Combo: If used after Feint or the Disengage action, the AP cost of this is reduced by 2.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Athletics, Stealth, Deception, Roguecraft, Perception, Insight, Negotiation, Intimidation or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Agility.

L8.name: Spellsteal
L8.description: You attempt to steal an effect from the target, removing it from the target and applying it to yourself for the same duration, provided you meet the requirements (if any). You must pay mana equal to the effects original mana cost. Effects granted through stances, transformations and permanent effects cannot be stolen. After stealing an effect, you can’t use this again until the stolen effect has ended. The stolen effect also cannot be re-applied on the target until it has faded from you. You must have seen the effect be applied or otherwise identified an effect before you can steal it.

L1.name: Timberwright's Core Crafting
L1.description: You may use the Timberwright's Core Crafting abilities You may craft using Ash and Yew materials.

L2.name: Stable Foundation
L2.description: Make a crafting check, afterwards all future crafting dice for this session cannot roll below a 3.

L3.name: Strengthening the Bond
L3.description: You swap places with a willing ally. This movement does not trigger any attacks of opportunity. You cannot use this ability if it causes an ally unit to occupy an invalid space. Before or after the swap you can take a 5ft step, but the swap must occur. You may spend either 0 AP or 1 RP to use this ability.

L4.name: Expertise Study (Carpentry)
L4.description: You gain 5 Expertise Points in 3 different carpentry crafts.  You also gain wielding proficiency in one carpentry weapon of your choice or artifice chainsaws.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Carpentry.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power, Focus or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Beaver Timber Fang
L8.description: Make a normal heavy attack against the target. Afterwards make a light attack against the target with Sure Hit that immediately renders the target prone, even if the damage is reduced to 0.

L1.name: Thefts of the past
L1.description: When making a check with the Roguecraft skill, you may treat the object or person you’re interacting with as being up to an hour in the past. This may allow you to steal things not currently carried, open doors before they were locked and more. Does not work in combat and an item that is consumed cannot be stolen. Using the Delay encounter start action does not consume your encounter start action use and you may use it multiple times.

L2.name: Start Dash
L2.description: You move up to your speed.

L3.name: Steal Time
L3.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you inflict Time Stolen , which lowers their Initiative by 1 and you gain Stolen Time , which increases yours by 1. This effect can stack both on you and on enemies.

L4.name: Shatter Time
L4.description: For this encounter you may act during different parts of a round. When you end your turn with AP remaining, you may choose to act immediately after another creature ends their turn. You do not regain AP or RP and these extra actions are not considered turns. You may take these extra actions multiple times during a round, provided you have AP remaining. You cannot use Setup abilities during this extra actions.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Cunning or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Agility.

L8.name: Secret Art: Rewind
L8.description: You rewind time to before your last action. All results based on your last action are reversed- All HP, Mana, AP and RP are returned to how they were. The ability you used in that action is no longer considered used, but this ability is.

L1.name: Rolling Thunder
L1.description: After moving at least 40ft in a turn, your next unarmed strike that turn gains a +2 Transcendent Lightning bonus to accuracy and damage.

L2.name: Heavenly Lightning Stance
L2.description: You enter the Heavenly Lightning Stance, a stance that lets you move with the unpredictability of the heavens. For your first movement of your turn, you may pass through enemies and your movement becomes a Sidestep. If you have mastered Sky Talon Style, entering this stance also counts as your Wind Stance. This stance lasts until you enter a different stance or make an attack with anything other than a melee attack or an ability with the Unarmed Strike keyword.

L3.name: Lightning Lariat
L3.description: You gather lightning energy and move up to your speed in a direct line and make a Heavy attack against a target. If you moved more than 60ft before making the attack, your attack gains the Trick Attack keyword. Overcharge: You may spend X mana to move an additional 2 x X times your speed.  Combo : If used after Heavenly Lightning Stance or Secret Art: Lightning Release, the mana cost is reduced by 1.

L4.name: Living Lightning
L4.description: You turn into a spark of lightning as you move. For this movement action, you deal Light Lightning damage to all creatures you pass through.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Intimidation, Survival or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power or Agility.

L8.name: Secret Art: Lightning Release
L8.description: You infuse yourself with Lightning and gain Lightning Release until the end of the encounter, allowing you to deal Lightning damage with your unarmed attacks and immediately enter the Heavenly Lightning Stance. While this lasts, you also gain the following benefits:  Your movement speed is doubled. You can make an additional attack when you use Double Punch.  When making an unarmed attack, your critical threshold is lowered by 1 (19-20 base). Whenever you hit with a critical hit with an unarmed attack, the attack arcs to a different enemy within 20ft of the original target. This attack uses the same attack roll and damage, but cannot arc again. If you ever leave the Lightning Stance, you lose the benefit of this.

L1.name: Transmutation Basics
L1.description: You gain Transmuter Core Crafting Abilities. You may use one of these abilities in a crafting session. If you use one of these abilities, other transmuters can no longer use their co craft abilities with you. Similarly, if a co crafter uses one of these abilities for you, you can no longer use any of these abilities. You can craft using non-advanced materials and base materials. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times. You can now craft Elixirs, Potions, Flasks and Salves. You craft 3 of the same Elixir, Potion, Flask or Salve when using an interlude point instead of 1. You only need to pay the listed cost once.

L2.name: Transmute Weapon
L2.description: Using the available materials around you, you transmute any basic weapon or shield into your hand or next to an ally, your ally can grab the weapon at the time of its creation for 0 RP, as well as sheathe their weapons.  The weapon lasts until the end of the encounter and is destroyed. You can transmute weapons out of special material if there is enough of it in the area, or if you have one (such as an ingot) but the material is destroyed afterwards.  The DM determines if this is possible in the specific case.

L3.name: Transmute Wall
L3.description: You quickly transmute multiple walls to defend against an incoming ranged attack.   Each wall you summon reduces the damage of the incoming attack by 2* Focus, you can summon as many walls as you like against the incoming attack for 1 mana per wall. After the attack resolves, every wall you made vanishes regardless of remaining HP.

L4.name: Deconstruction
L4.description: By spending an Interlude Point, you deconstruct a basic item into materials. You gain 500 matching crafting material per ingot or block used in the item. If the item does not use ingots, you instead get 1 matching crafting material per clim value of the item. Regardless of the material used in the item, you will only gain the base price amount even if using upgraded materials and all alloys and upgrades are lost.  You can deconstruct up to 5 basic items for 1 interlude point. Alternatively instead of 1 basic item, you can transmute up to 1000u of random junk into up to 250u of usable basic (ie: Iron, Pine etc) Blacksmithing Materials, Armorsmithing Materials, Carpentry Materials, Calligraphy Material, Magical Fuel Materials or Medical Materials units at a 4:1 ratio. You can also use Deconstruction as an Errand Action, but the deconstruction limit is reduced to 1 basic item instead of 5 basic items.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain 5 Transmuter points. Pick one of the crafting disciplines, such as blacksmithing or alchemy. Whenever you use your Transmuter abilities in a craft of that discipline, you may add your transmuter points as bonus to that ability (even if it's not a roll).

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness. In addition, as an errand you can use Co-Craft to help in crafting once.  Only 1 person can Co-Craft with a craft at a time. When you Co-Craft, you help another crafter allowing you to use a single Co-Craft ability in their crafting session. Your Co-Craft actions count towards the 1 limit of using an ability for the main crafter.  (Both you and the main crafter cannot use the same co-craft ability in one crafting session). You must declare you are helping before any crafting actions are taken by the main crafter.

L8.name: Transmute Terrain
L8.description: Creates a connected transmuted terrain that grows from the point of origin.  For every mana spent, you can create up to 4 5ft squares of terrain that must be connected.  Choose solid ground normal terrain or difficult terrain.  Removes effects on the terrain such as ice, fire. The terrain can push objects and creatures in the direction its being created.  If the transmute terrain deals damage to a creature, the spell stops after and the 5ft segment that contacted them is destroyed.  Each segment of wall has HP equal to 20+Focus Heroics caught in the terrain may spend 1 RP to step out of it. Once they do, they can't be shoved within this use of transmute terrain even if you attempt to hit them with it again.

L1.name: Trap Basics
L1.description: You may convert 100u of junk into a Trapper's Trap you can use for your abilities. You may do so as a Rest action or as an Errand action. Trapper's Traps are a special item that cost 1 burden. You may carry up to 3 Trapper's Traps without them costing Burden. When resting at your camp outdoors, you may set traps that will alert you to enemies approaching by using a rest action. These do not consume a Trapper's Trap,  but allow you to use Survival to detect enemies instead of Perception. After each of such rests, you obtain 1d10 x 5 food units.

L2.name: Bear Trap
L2.description: You place down a bear trap in an empty square within range. If an enemy enters that square, they trigger the trap taking Light full pierce damage and becoming rooted until they spend 2 AP to remove the effect. Against beasts, this does Heavy damage instead of Light damage. This ability consumes a Trap.

L3.name: Place Trap
L3.description: You choose to spend either 1 minute or 1 hour to place an undetermined trap in an area. You may only have 2 traps placed at a time in this way. When an enemy comes within 20ft of you, you may spend 0 RP to determine the effects of a trap and trigger it. If you spent 1 minute, you may choose a Simple Trap. If you spent 1 hour, you may choose either a Simple Trap or a Complex Trap. Log Swing (Simple): The target takes Light bludgeoning damage and is knocked back 20ft. Snare (Simple) : You place up to 3 snare traps, each within 20ft of each other. When triggered, the target’s movement is interrupted and they fall prone. Net (Complex): The target is caught in a net and raised up into the air. It remains rooted there until the net is destroyed by dealing 15 damage, after which the target falls prone on the ground below it. Pitfall (Complex): The target falls down a 5x5, 20ft deep pit and takes falling damage as normal. A used trap cannot be reused. Setting a trap consumes a Trapper's Trap.

L4.name: Ambush Trapper
L4.description: You may use Place Trap’s 0 RP effect once this encounter, even if you didn’t spend the time to set it up. The GM may rule that the pitfall and net options are unavailable based on the situation. These activations still cost a Trap when used (but not if you don't use them).

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain 5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Stealth, Medicine, Perception, Survival, Animal Husbandry or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Cunning or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power or Agility. The food units received from your key ability is increased to 1d10 x 10 food units.

L8.name: Secret Art: Guiding Shot
L8.description: You may make a Heavy attack against the target. The target may spend 1 RP to avoid the attack. If they do, you may Sidestep them up to 20ft in a direction of your choice and then trigger a Place Trap effect, provided you have at least one set up. This trap effect does not count towards your per encounter limitation. You may not Sidestep an enemy into a hazard (such as off a cliff or into fire) apart from your trap, unless the target has nowhere else to go. Alternatively, you may choose to move an enemy into a Bear Trap you set up within the 20ft movement instead of a Place Trap effect. You can only choose one of these two options, not both.

L1.name: Perfect Mimicry
L1.description: When given enough preparation time and enough knowledge about the subject, you are able to perfectly impersonate another person (GM’s discretion on what is ‘enough’). When disguised in this way, other people are unable to see through your disguise through normal means unless you act or say things that are strange enough to draw suspicions. This disguise is partially magical in nature, but cannot make up for obvious size or shape differences.

L2.name: Assign Decoy
L2.description: Choose one willing ally other than yourself within range and one enemy within range. The enemy is Challenged by the chosen ally until the start of your next turn.

L3.name: Swap
L3.description: You Swap positions with a chosen willing ally or any non-heroic enemy. You may use either AP or RP for this ability. You don't need to pay the cost of both.

L4.name: Misdirected Blow
L4.description: You may take the Dodge action. If the attack is successfully avoided, the attack is then made against an enemy within 5ft of you and using the same accuracy roll. This enemy must be different from the one making the attack. The new target may react to this attack as normal. Does not work against Secret Art or Esoteric Art level attacks.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Deception.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Agility.

L8.name: Secret Art: The Grand Theater
L8.description: All allies within range may sidestep up to their movement speed. You may Sidestep all enemies within range up to their movement speed. After all targets have moved, everyone that was originally in range of this ability immediately gains 2 AP they must spend on attacks against other creatures if possible.

L1.name: Improved Death Perception
L1.description: You can see the death of non-living things allowing you to automatically do maximum, full pierce damage against objects. You apply 1 Mark of Death every time you hit an enemy with ‘Lines of Death’ or ‘Point of Death’ up to a maximum of 7 marks.

L2.name: Cut Away
L2.description: Instead of rolling a save normally, you may make a Precise Attack against the Potency of the ability instead. If your accuracy roll beats the Potency, you are treated as having passed the save.

L3.name: Point of Death II
L3.description: You may make a Heavy Attack against the target. This attack deals maximum damage instead of rolling for the damage. On killing blow, refund the mana and AP cost spent on this ability.

L4.name: Cut Magic
L4.description: You make a Heavy Attack against a temporary magic effect. If your accuracy roll is equal or higher than the Potency of the effect, the effect is removed. Overcharge: You may spend 1 additional AP to turn the Heavy attack into a Precise attack instead.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Stealth, Medicine, Perception, Insight or Common Knowledge.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Power.

L8.name: Secret Art: 7 Points of Death
L8.description: You make a number of Precise Attacks up to the amount of Marks of Death on the target. Each attack deals damage equal to your Focus + Power instead of rolling for damage.

L1.name: Vampiric Strength
L1.description: Your unarmed attack damage is treated as a One-Handed Weapon in which you are proficient. Your unarmed attacks cannot deal non-lethal damage. You may use the Vampire Bite ability.

L2.name: Vampiric Fascination
L2.description: You charm a non-heroic creature within range. While charmed, the target must spend its AP during its turns on moving towards you in the most direct path possible while avoiding any dangers it is aware of. If it is already next to you, it instead takes no actions. At the start of your next turn, you may spend 4 AP to continue the charm. If so, the charm continues until you stop paying the AP cost or until the target takes damage from an attack other than a Vampire Bite. While you have a target under the influence of Vampiric Fascination, your Vampire Bite costs 0 AP and has Sure Hit plus Full Pierce against that target the first time you use it each turn.

L3.name: Vampire Shapeshift
L3.description: You are able to change your form to that of a rat or bat. While in this form, your size changes to that of the chosen animal and you are treated as a normal animal of that type. You cannot make any attacks or use any abilities while in this form. You may exit this form at any time for 0 RP.

L4.name: Cloak of Bats
L4.description: You vanish into a swarm of bats and reappear somewhere else within range. If this was used in reaction to an attack, that attack automatically misses you.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Blood Frenzy
L8.description: You go into a blood frenzy, mindlessly attacking enemies near you. While the blood frenzy lasts, you must attack the nearest enemy target with a basic unarmed attack. If two targets are equally close, you may choose which one you attack. If no enemies are within range, you must use your movement to move closer to the closest visible enemy until you can attack them. While under the effect of blood frenzy, you heal for half the damage you deal with your unarmed attacks. You cannot use Vampire Bite while under the effect of Blood Frenzy. This effect lasts until all visible enemies are dead or until your mana runs out. At the start of your turn, you lose 2 mana.

L1.name: Lord's Dominion
L1.description: You can partially resist the effect of the sun. You may now spend 1 round in the sun before you start taking damage. Spending a turn outside of the sun resets this effect, giving you another round where you do not suffer the negative effects. You gain +1 Vampire Lord bonus to Presence.

L2.name: Bloodbolt
L2.description: You make a Light attack against the target. Overcharge : You may spend X HP to increase the damage the attack deals by X. X may not exceed your Toughness.

L3.name: Feeding Frenzy
L3.description: You may use this after reducing an enemy’s hit points to 0 or less. You may immediately use your Vampire Bite ability for 0 RP against the enemy you downed.

L4.name: Vampiric Domination
L4.description: You mentally dominate the target. The target immediately considers you their master and they will serve you like a loyal servant. You may command them and they will obey most commands. Any command that is obviously suicidal or completely goes against their nature (for example: telling a servant to jump off a cliff or to kill their own child) will end the domination prematurely. This effect lasts until dispelled, they die or until you use Vampiric Domination on another target. After the effect ends, the target will remember everything it was forced to do while it was your servant and it cannot be affected by Vampiric Domination again for a year.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on any non crafting or gathering skill.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Bloody Ritual
L8.description: You create a blood tether to all enemies within range, causing them to be Slowed. The tether lasts until the start of your next turn or until they move out of the range of this ability. At the start of your next turn, the tethers explode, dealing Heavy damage to all tethered enemies as it sucks the life energy from them. For every enemy dealt damage with this ability, you recover 1 Mana or 5 HP (your choice). If an enemy dies while under the effect of the tether but before it explodes, you also gain this regeneration effect.

L1.name: Emergency Shields
L1.description: You may cast Power Word: Shield and Power Word: Guardian Shield (when obtained) by spending 1 RP instead of 2 AP.

L2.name: Shield Burst
L2.description: You heal the target for an amount equal to half the temporary HP they have, then all temporary HP on them is removed. All enemies within 5ft of the target must make a save against your Potency or be pushed back 10ft. You may use 1 AP instead of 1 RP for this ability.

L3.name: Redistribute
L3.description: You may transfer temporary HP from a single willing ally within range to any number of other allies within range. The total amount of temporary HP must be the same after the transfer. May only redistribute temporary HP you applied through the use of a Shieldwarden or Veilguard ability. Does not affect temporary HP added through Reinforce.

L4.name: Purifying Shields
L4.description: You remove all stacks of a debuff from every ally within range that has temporary HP.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Magic, Religion, Medicine, Perception, Negotiation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Power or Agility.

L8.name: Power Word: Guardian Shield
L8.description: The target gains temporary HP until the end of the encounter equal to 11 + 2 x Power. While this temporary HP lasts, on damage effects do not trigger on them unless the damage dealt does damage to their actual HP. If this source of temporary HP is overwritten by a stronger source, this effect is lost.

L1.name: Toxic Blast
L1.description: You may use Toxic Blast.

L2.name: Poison Spray
L2.description: You make a Light attack against all targets in a 60ft wide, 30ft long cone. On damage, you inflict the enemy with Toxic, making them take 1d6 full pierce Poison damage at the start of their turn until they spend 2 AP to remove the poison effect.

L3.name: Catalyze
L3.description: The target must succeed at a Save. On failure, you inflict Stagger II or Sunder II until the end of your next turn.

L4.name: Resistant Venom
L4.description: Your Poison effects gain Persist 1.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Stealth, Deception, Roguecraft, Medicine, Survival or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus.

L8.name: Crippling Poison
L8.description: You slow the target for their movement.  Overcharge : You may spend 1 additional mana to make it affect a movement not reliant on movement speed, something that moves a fixed distance. The distance moved with that is halved. If that movement had an attack attached that now cannot be made, the ability is NOT refunded.

L1.name: Envenom Weapon
L1.description: You may use Envenom Weapon.

L2.name: Venom Strike
L2.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, inflicts the enemy with Toxic, making them take 1d6 full pierce Poison damage at the start of their turn until they spend 2 AP to remove the poison effect.

L3.name: Deadly Venom
L3.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, you worsen the target’s Toxic, letting it stack and adding +1 stack to it. Stacks a maximum of 3 times. A Toxic with more than 1 stack deals damage at the end of the target’s turn rather than the start.

L4.name: Maximum Dose
L4.description: You maximize the damage they take from your Toxic.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Stealth, Deception, Roguecraft, Medicine, Survival or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Cunning.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus.

L8.name: Outbreak
L8.description: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, inflicts the enemy with Toxic, making them take 1d6 full pierce Poison damage at the start of their turn until they spend 2 AP to remove the poison effect. If the target is already suffering from Toxic, instead immediately trigger the damage effect from it.

L1.name: Disable Magic
L1.description: When using Decipher Magic, you are able to disable both tier 1 and tier 2 level bounded fields. You are able to disable or change who is excluded from your own Bounded Fields for 0 RP.

L2.name: Alarm Ward
L2.description: A ward that can be placed on a normal-sized room or can be combined to ward off a house or larger area. Each ward can cover a 40ft radius. For each Alarm Ward placed in the same area as another Alarm Ward, this radius increases by 40ft (For example, 120ft with 3 Alarm Wards). If someone other than you passes through the ward, the ward triggers creating a loud ringing noise that persists until the ward is disabled. Casting this takes 10 minutes of uninterrupted casting. Once triggered, the field remains active for 5 more minutes before vanishing. At the time of casting, you may choose people that can pass through the barrier without triggering it. If not triggered, Alarm Ward lasts until you remove it. For each Alarm Ward you maintain, your maximum mana is reduced by 1. This is considered a tier 1 ward.

L3.name: Hidden Wards
L3.description: You may choose to hide your Bounded Fields when casting them by making a Stealth check. The casted ward uses the result of your Stealth check. Hidden Bounded Fields cannot be seen with Sense Magic without a successful perception or insight check.

L4.name: Explosive Ward
L4.description: A ward that can be placed on a door or medium-sized container of some sort. If other than you knowingly tries to open the door or container in any way (whether normal opening, attacking it etc), the ward triggers creating a loud noise and dealing Heavy Fire Damage to all creatures in a 10ft radius around the container. All items inside the container (if any) also take this damage. Once triggered, the bounded field vanishes. Casting this takes 10 minutes of uninterrupted casting. You may at the time of casting choose a number of people equal to your Focus that can also open the container or door without triggering the ward. If not triggered, Explosive Ward lasts until you remove it. For each Explosive Ward you maintain your maximum mana is reduced by 2. This is considered a tier 2 ward.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Stealth, Magic, Perception or Insight or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Cunning, Reason or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Sapping Ward
L8.description: A ward that can be placed on a normal-sized room or can be combined to ward off a house or larger area. Each ward can cover a 20ft radius and for each Sapping Ward placed in the same area as another Sapping Ward, this radius increases by 20ft (For example, 60ft with 3 Sapping Wards) If someone other than you enters the area, they must make a save against your Potency or become Weakened. Non-heroic units that enter the area must make a save or become Stunned instead. These conditions last while they remain inside the field and for an additional 2 rounds after exiting it. Casting this takes 10 minutes of uninterrupted casting. Once triggered, the field remains active for 5 more minutes before vanishing. You may at the time of casting choose a number of people equal to your Focus that can enter the area without triggering the ward. If not triggered, Sapping Ward lasts until you remove it. While it remains, your maximum mana is reduced by 2 for each instance of Sapping Ward you have active. This is considered a tier 2 ward.

L1.name: Mana Sight
L1.description: You may use Mana Sight. One of your eyes becomes an Life Perception Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type. When making an attack with the Mystic Eye keyword that use the Life Perception eye, you ignore 1 point of guard.

L2.name: Two Point Strike
L2.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you apply Wave Palm I.  Wave Palm increases the mana cost of the afflicted target’s next action that uses mana by an amount equal to the amount of stacks applied. If the triggering attack makes the number of total stacks exceed the target's current mana, they lose mana equal to the amount of total stacks and take true damage equal to triple this amount. Wave Palm does not stack with itself, use only the higher value. If the target has no mana, they immediately take true damage as if their stacks exceeded their current mana. When the Wave Palm effect triggers, either by increasing the mana cost or by dealing the true damage, the Wave Palm effect is removed.

L3.name: Mystic Eyes of Life Perception
L3.description: You temporarily empower your eyes. While active, you gain 360 degree vision and automatically detect all enemies within a 30ft radius of you that are not behind at least 1ft of full cover. In addition, you can see the approximate HP and Mana percentage of all enemies in this range. This lasts for 5 minutes or until you turn it off.

L4.name: Disrupt Ki
L4.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, if they have any Concentration ability active, that ability immediately ends as if they had chosen to drop it.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Athletics, Negotiation, Perception or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus.

L8.name: Four Point Strike
L8.description: You may make a Light Attack against the target. On damage, you apply Wave Palm II.  Combo: If used after Two Point Strike or against a target afflicted with Wave Palm I, the mana cost is reduced by 2 and the AP cost by 1. If the target is afflicted with Wave Palm I, you apply Wave Palm III instead of Wave Palm II. Wave Palm increases the mana cost of the afflicted target’s next action that uses mana by an amount equal to the amount of stacks applied. If the triggering attack makes the number of total stacks exceed the target's current mana, they lose mana equal to the amount of total stacks and take true damage equal to triple this amount. Wave Palm does not stack with itself, use only the higher value. If the target has no mana, they immediately take true damage as if their stacks exceeded their current mana. When the Wave Palm effect triggers, either by increasing the mana cost or by dealing the true damage, the Wave Palm effect is removed.

L1.name: Expanded Menagerie
L1.description: You may spend 1 mana with using Summon Animal to choose which animal you summon. You add the Cheetah and Landshark to the pool of animals you can summon.

L2.name: X-Attack
L2.description: You give your summon a +1 Focus and +1 Power buff.

L3.name: Quick Revive
L3.description: You immediately resummon the summon from your Summon Animal that was killed in this current encounter. If multiple of your summons have died, you summon the one that died most recently.

L4.name: Leashing
L4.description: You teleport your summoned animal to an empty square within 5ft of you.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend on Animal Husbandry.  You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Fitness, Cunning, Reason, Awareness or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: Bestial Wrath
L8.description: Your summon gains Bestial Wrath and AP equal to its maximum AP they may immediately use. Bestial Wrath gives +2 Focus and +2 Power and prevents the summon from dropping below 1 HP. This effect lasts for 3 rounds or until the summon is reduced to 1 HP.

L1.name: Wind Master
L1.description: You gain Wind Mastery. You can use Glide.

L2.name: Gale-Wind Aura
L2.description: You project a Gale-Wind Aura around yourself that increases the movement speed of allies (excluding yourself) within 60ft of you by 20ft. An ally that starts their movement in the Aura, keeps the Speed increase for that move even if they leave the aura. Upkeep: You must spend 1 AP at the start of each turn or the aura fades.

L3.name: Tailwind
L3.description: You and all allies within 40ft gain Tailwind, which provides a +1 bonus to initiative and doubles movement speed in the first round of combat.

L4.name: Headwind
L4.description: You project an aura around you that hinders the movements of enemies.  All enemies that start their movement within 30ft of you become slowed. If an enemy takes a Move basic action outside of your zone and spends more than 1 AP, they are still slowed after calculating each individual AP spent on the move. Upkeep: 1 AP

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason or Fitness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Agility.

L8.name: Fly
L8.description: You or the creature you touch gains Fly , which gives them controlled Flight, Hover and their movement speed is doubled while flying until the end of the encounter. If they take damage while flying higher than 5ft above the ground, their altitude drops by 20ft. If this would reduce their altitude to the point where they touch the ground, they become rooted and cannot fly again until the end of their next turn. If the attack that reduced your HP came from an flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude.

L1.name: Rapid Blasts
L1.description: You may use Ki Blast II.

L2.name: Flying Kick
L2.description: You may Sidestep next to the target within range and make a Light Attack against it. You gain Flight for this movement. Combo: Your Strike of Still Water has its AP cost reduced by 1.

L3.name: Ki Burst
L3.description: Your attack deals an extra X damage. If used with a basic attack or double punch, deal 2X extra damage instead up to a maximum of +10 damage. Only affects 1 damage roll even if an attack makes multiple attacks.

L4.name: Redirected Force
L4.description: You redirect the incoming attack back onto the attacker, using the same attack roll. The attacker may react to this attack as normal. Can not be used against the same attack used to enter the Serene Water stance.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 Fitness or Awareness.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 Focus, Power, Agility or Toughness.

L8.name: Secret Art: True Serenity
L8.description: You enter the Serene Water stance and gain an additional +17 bonus to your Evasion until the start of your next turn.

L1.name: Wind Master
L1.description: You gain Wind Mastery. You can use Glide.

L2.name: Wind Infusion
L2.description: You surround the willing target in protective winds, changing their typing to Wind II. As a Wind II type, they take regular damage from all other elements, but only half damage from Wind or Earth damage or from attacks with the Wind or Earth keyword unless that attack deals true damage. They take 50% more damage from Frost damage or attacks with the Frost keyword. While the ward lasts, they gain a +2 bonus to their Evasion against ranged weapon attacks. The ward lasts until the end of the encounter or until their type is overwritten by a more powerful type.

L3.name: Wind Wall
L3.description: You create a wall of wind that is 30ft long, 5ft wide and 10ft high that slows down all ranged weapon attacks passing through it, treating them as having only half the normal range. This wall must be a straight line. These attacks also lose the Lock On, Sure Hit, Half Pierce, Guard Crush and Full Pierce keywords. In addition, the targets of those attacks are treated as having +2 Evasion and +1 Guard. Any Spells that are considered attacks (those that require an accuracy roll against a person that isn’t using the Block reaction) that pass through the wall also suffer this penalty.

L4.name: Wind-Empowered Aim
L4.description: You project Wind-Empowered Aim aura around yourself that empowers the ranged attacks of your allies. When a heroic ally within 20ft of you (including yourself) makes a ranged attack, they may add 1d4 to the accuracy roll. Upkeep: You must spend 1 AP at the start of each turn or the Aura fades.

L5.name: Skills
L5.description: You gain +5 skill points in Medicine, Magic, Religion, History, Flight, Artifice or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

L6.name: Heart
L6.description: You gain +1 to Reason or Presence.

L7.name: Soul
L7.description: You gain +1 to Focus or Agility.

L8.name: Wind Shield
L8.description: You grant the target  1 RP that must be spent on the basic Dodge or Block action against the triggering attack as well as Wind Shield . Wind Shield grants a bonus to Dodge equal to your Focus against the triggering attack. The triggering attack can also no longer benefit from Lock On or Sure Hit
